1217	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
1218	
1219	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
1220	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
1221	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
1222	
1223	Blueprint reference: `def:cohen_macaulay_local` (Stacks tag 00N4). -/
1224	
1225	/-- A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth
1226	equals its Krull dimension: `depth(R) = dim R`.
1227	
1228	Encoded as a `Prop`-valued type class so downstream consumers can write
1229	`[CohenMacaulay R]` and use Cohen–Macaulay as a hypothesis. Mathlib at the
1230	pinned commit (`b80f227`) does not expose any Cohen–Macaulay predicate;
1231	this is the upstream gap-fill.
1232	
1233	iter-176+: the predicate is `Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R =
1234	ringKrullDim R`. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the carrier definition is a
1235	typed `sorry` at the `Prop` level — substantively, the predicate is the
1236	named equality, but we package it as a `class` so use sites are uniform. -/
1237	class CohenMacaulay (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1238	    [IsNoetherianRing R] : Prop where
1239	  /-- The Cohen–Macaulay equation: `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`. The numeric
1240	  comparison is in `WithBot ℕ∞` after coercion of the `ℕ∞`-valued depth. -/
1241	  depth_eq_krullDim :
1242	    (Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R
1243	
1244	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
1245	
1246	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
1247	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
1248	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
1249	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
1250	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
1251	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
1252	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
1253	
1254	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
1255	
1256	namespace CohenMacaulay
1257	
1258	/-! ### Helper 1 (axiom-clean): length-bound on regular sequences
1259	
1260	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, every `R`-regular sequence has length at most
1261	`ringKrullDim R`. This is the **upper bound** half of Stacks 00OD: it is the
1262	specialisation of the equality
1263	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) + rs.length = ringKrullDim R`
1264	(`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) to the observation that
1265	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) ≥ 0` whenever the quotient is nontrivial, which it
1266	is precisely because `IsRegular` rules out `rs • ⊤ = ⊤`.
1267	
1268	iter-181 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. -/
1269	private lemma length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular
1270	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1271	    {rs : List R} (h : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs) :
1272	    (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim R := by
1273	  have heq := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs h
1274	  have hntq : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) := by
1275	    rw [Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
1276	    intro habs
1277	    apply h.top_ne_smul
1278	    change (⊤ : Submodule R R) = (Ideal.ofList rs) • ⊤
1279	    rw [habs]; simp
1280	  have hnn : (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) :=
1281	    ringKrullDim_nonneg_of_nontrivial
1282	  calc (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞)
1283	      = 0 + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by simp
1284	    _ ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by gcongr
1285	    _ = ringKrullDim R := heq

1287	/-! ### Helper 1.5 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent-image of `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`

1289	For a local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`, the image of `x` in the

1295	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1296	    (x : R) (hx : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1297	    (hxnotsq : x ∉ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ^ 2) :
1298	    (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).toCotangent
1299	        (⟨x, hx⟩ : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Ideal R)) ≠ 0 := by

1301	  exact hxnotsq

1304	/-! ### Helper 2.0 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent dim-drop on `R ⧸ (x)`
1305	
1306	**Statement.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the
1307	cotangent space of `R / (x)` has dimension one less than that of `R`:
1308	```
1309	finrank κ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
1310	```
1311	where `κ = R / 𝔪` and `κ' = (R/(x)) / 𝔪'` are the two residue fields
1312	(canonically isomorphic via the natural quotient `R/𝔪 ≃ (R/(x))/𝔪'`).
1313	
1314	**Role.** This is the cotangent-space dim-drop building block for
1315	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` below: once Stacks 00NQ
1316	(regular local ⟹ domain) is filled in, this dim-drop is what upgrades
1317	`R/(x)` of dimension `k` back to `IsRegularLocalRing` via
1318	`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
1319	
1320	**Proof structure** (Bourbaki-style via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank`):
1321	1. The R-algebra map `π : R → R/(x)` induces a κ-linear surjection of
1322	   cotangent spaces `f : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent` via
1323	   `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq` (which applies because
1324	   `(maximalIdeal R).comap π = ker π ⊔ maximalIdeal R = (x) ⊔ 𝔪 = 𝔪`).
1325	2. The kernel of `f` is the R-submodule generated by the image `x̄` of `x`
1326	   in `𝔪.Cotangent` (via `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` — the
1327	   kernel is `(Submodule.comap 𝔪.subtype ((x) ⊓ 𝔪)).map 𝔪.toCotangent`
1328	   which collapses to `Submodule.span R {x̄}` because `(x) ⊆ 𝔪`).
1329	3. The R-action on 𝔪.Cotangent factors through κ (it's a κ-vector space),
1330	   so this is also `Submodule.span κ {x̄}`.
1331	4. `x ∉ 𝔪²` ⟺ `x̄ ≠ 0` (Helper 1.5), giving
1332	   `finrank κ (Submodule.span κ {x̄}) = 1` via `finrank_span_singleton`.
1333	5. Combining via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` and the
1334	   `LinearMap.quotKerEquivOfSurjective` isomorphism, we get the formula.
1335	   The residue-field switch `κ → κ'` is handled by
1336	   `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`, which
1337	   reduces the κ-finrank to spanFinrank (a ring-theoretic invariant) and
1338	   the spanFinrank-equality is purely numeric.
1339	
1340	**iter-187 deliverable**: structural scaffold with the assembled body left
1341	as a single named typed sorry (the κ-subspace identification step). The
1342	preparatory lemma `toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq` lands axiom-clean.
1343	
1344	**iter-188+** body fill-in: instantiate steps (1)-(5) above. Key Mathlib API:
1345	* `Ideal.mapCotangent`, `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq`,
1346	  `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)
1347	* `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)
1348	* `finrank_span_singleton` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)

1350	  (Mathlib b80f227 ✓) — bridges κ-finrank and κ'-finrank via spanFinrank.

1517	              (Submodule.span _ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R))) := rfl
1518	        _ ≤ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R)).ncard :=
1519	            Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite
1520	              ((V.erase v₀).finite_toSet.image _)

1521	        _ ≤ (V.erase v₀ : Set R).ncard :=
1522	            Set.ncard_image_le (V.erase v₀).finite_toSet
1523	        _ = (V.erase v₀).card := Set.ncard_coe_finset _
1524	        _ = V.card - 1 := Finset.card_erase_of_mem hv₀_V

1526	    omega
1527	  · -- (≥): (𝔪 R).spanFinrank ≤ (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1.
1528	    -- Lift-and-cons strategy: a min gen set T of 𝔪 (R/(x)) lifts to T_lift ⊆
1529	    -- 𝔪 R via `Function.surjInv` of `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the union
1530	    -- `T_lift ∪ {x}` generates 𝔪 R since 𝔪 R = (Ideal.span {x}) ⊔ (lift of 𝔪').
1531	    classical
1532	    set R' : Type u := R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} with hR'_def
1533	    let mkx : R →+* R' := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
1534	    let g : R' → R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1535	    have hg : ∀ y, mkx (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
1536	    -- Get min gen finset of 𝔪'.
1537	    have h𝔪'_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').FG := Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
1538	    obtain ⟨T, hT_card, hT_span⟩ :=
1539	      Submodule.FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank h𝔪'_fg
1540	    -- T : Finset R', T.card = spanFinrank 𝔪', span R' T = 𝔪'.
1541	    let T_lift : Finset R := T.image g
1542	    let U : Finset R := insert x T_lift
1543	    -- Step A: U generates 𝔪 R via the comap identification.
1544	    -- Comap mkx (𝔪 R') = 𝔪 R, since R is local and mkx is surjective.
1545	    have hcomap_eq : Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') =
1546	        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
1547	      have hmax : (Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R')).IsMaximal :=
1548	        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1549	      exact (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax
1550	    have hker_eq : RingHom.ker mkx = Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.mk_ker
1551	    -- Step B: 𝔪 R = (Ideal.span T_lift) ⊔ (Ideal.span {x}).
1552	    -- Compute: comap mkx (map mkx (Ideal.span T_lift)) = Ideal.span T_lift ⊔ ker mkx.
1553	    have hmap_T_lift : Ideal.map mkx (Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R)) =
1554	        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R' := by
1555	      rw [Ideal.map_span]
1556	      -- mkx '' T_lift = T (as sets).
1557	      have him : mkx '' (T_lift : Set R) = (T : Set R') := by
1558	        ext y
1559	        simp only [Set.mem_image, Finset.coe_image, T_lift]
1560	        refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
1561	        · rintro ⟨a, ⟨b, hbT, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
1562	          rw [hg b]; exact hbT
1563	        · intro hyT
1564	          exact ⟨g y, ⟨y, hyT, rfl⟩, hg y⟩
1565	      rw [him]; exact hT_span
1566	    have h𝔪R_decomp : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R =
1567	        Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by
1568	      calc IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R
1569	          = Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') := hcomap_eq.symm
1570	        _ = Ideal.comap mkx (Ideal.map mkx (Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R))) := by
1571	              rw [hmap_T_lift]
1572	        _ = Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ RingHom.ker mkx := by
1573	              rw [Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective' mkx Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective]
1574	        _ = Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by rw [hker_eq]
1575	    -- Step C: span R U = Ideal.span T_lift ⊔ Ideal.span {x}.
1576	    have hU_span_eq : Submodule.span R (↑U : Set R) =
1577	        Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by
1578	      have hUeq : (↑U : Set R) = (↑T_lift : Set R) ∪ {x} := by
1579	        change ((insert x T_lift : Finset R) : Set R) = _
1580	        rw [Finset.coe_insert, Set.insert_eq, Set.union_comm]
1581	      rw [hUeq, Submodule.span_union]
1582	    -- Step D: spanFinrank 𝔪 R ≤ U.card ≤ T.card + 1.
1583	    calc Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1584	        = Submodule.spanFinrank (Submodule.span R (↑U : Set R)) := by
1585	          rw [hU_span_eq, ← h𝔪R_decomp]
1586	      _ ≤ (↑U : Set R).ncard :=
1587	          Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite U.finite_toSet
1588	      _ = U.card := by simp
1589	      _ ≤ T_lift.card + 1 := by
1590	          have := Finset.card_insert_le x T_lift
1591	          simpa [U] using this
1592	      _ ≤ T.card + 1 := by
1593	          have hle : T_lift.card ≤ T.card := Finset.card_image_le
1594	          omega
1595	      _ = Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') + 1 := by
1596	          rw [hT_card]

1598	/-! ### Helper 2 (substantive typed `sorry`): system-of-parameters as a regular sequence
1599	
1600	For a regular local ring `R` of Krull dimension `d = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`,
1601	a minimal generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of the maximal ideal `𝔪` is an `R`-regular
1602	sequence. This is the **lower bound** half of Stacks 00OD.
1603	
1604	The proof in Stacks 00OD uses:
1605	1. A regular local ring is an integral domain (Stacks 00NQ).
1606	2. For each `c < d`, the quotient `R / (x_1, …, x_c)` is again a regular local
1607	   ring, of Krull dimension `d - c` (Krull's principal ideal theorem +
1608	   `lemma-one-equation`).
1609	
1610	Step (1) — `IsRegularLocalRing R ⟹ IsDomain R` — is **not present in Mathlib at
1611	the pinned commit** (`b80f227`); a `lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is
1612	a domain" returns nothing relevant. Step (2) requires the regular-quotient
1613	inductive structure (Stacks 00NQ + height-one quotient regularity), which is
1614	similarly absent. Both are substantive multi-iter content.
1615	
1616	The signature here is the non-tautological existence statement: a list `rs : List R`
1617	with `rs.length = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`, `rs ⊆ maximalIdeal R`, and
1618	`IsRegular R rs`. Downstream, `of_regular` consumes this directly to close the
1619	`depth ≥ d` lower bound.
1620	
1621	iter-185 Lane G **STRUCTURAL SCAFFOLD**: the main lemma is structurally
1622	complete by strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`. The inductive step is
1623	factored into `regularLocal_inductive_step` (axiom-clean: lift-and-cons
1624	assembly via Mathlib's `IsRegular.cons'`) plus the Mathlib-gap helper
1625	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` (typed `sorry`,
1626	Stacks 00NQ + 00NU consolidated). The substrate work (`IsRegularLocalRing ⟹
1627	IsDomain` + Krull's PIT for quotient-by-minimal-generator) is genuinely
1628	multi-iter; the iter-185 progress is the structural extraction of the
1629	Mathlib gap into a narrow, well-typed helper, leaving the assembly
1630	axiom-clean for the gap-fill iteration to finish in `~5 LOC` once the
1631	helper closes. -/

1633	/-! ### Narrow substrate gap (Stacks 00NQ): regular local ring is a domain
1634	
1635	The Auslander–Buchsbaum-route closure of "regular local ring is Cohen–Macaulay"
1636	ultimately reduces to **Stacks 00NQ**: every regular local Noetherian ring
1637	is an integral domain. Mathlib at the pinned commit `b80f227` does not expose
1638	this implication (`lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is a domain"
1639	returns nothing relevant; `lean_loogle` for `IsRegularLocalRing _ → IsDomain _`
1640	returns empty).
1641	
1642	Stacks 00NQ proof sketch (induction on `dim R = spanFinrank 𝔪`):
1643	* Base `dim R = 0`: then `𝔪 = ⊥`, hence `R` is a field, hence a domain.
1644	* Step `dim R = d + 1 ≥ 1`:
1645	  - Pick `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` (axiom-clean via Nakayama; see
1646	    `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos` below).
1647	  - By the cotangent dim-drop (`finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ`,
1648	    iter-188 Lane G1 closure) **plus** Krull's principal ideal theorem
1649	    (`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim_of_mem_jacobson`
1650	    gives the `≥` half), `R / (x)` is regular local of dim `d`.
1651	  - By induction, `R / (x)` is a domain, hence `(x)` is a prime ideal of `R`.
1652	  - Let `𝔭` be a minimal prime of `R` with `𝔭 ⊆ (x)`. For `y ∈ 𝔭`, write
1653	    `y = xz`; since `𝔭` is prime and `x ∉ 𝔭` (else `x ∈ 𝔭 ⊆ (x)` doesn't
1654	    immediately give a contradiction but combined with `dim R/(x) = d` and
1655	    `x ∈ 𝔭` height-zero we get `𝔭 = 0` which is the conclusion), we get
1656	    `z ∈ 𝔭`. Hence `𝔭 ⊆ x 𝔭`, so iterating, `𝔭 ⊆ ∩ x^n 𝔭 ⊆ ∩ 𝔪^n = 0`
1657	    (Krull intersection). So `𝔭 = 0`, and `R` is a domain.
1658	
1659	Full formalization is ~300 LOC (involves Krull's PIT, prime avoidance,
1660	Krull intersection, minimal-primes-are-finite); deferred to a future iter
1661	or Mathlib upstream contribution. -/
1662	
1663	/-- **Axiom-clean Nakayama witness.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
1664	with `spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1`, there exists `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`.
1665	
1666	This is the "cotangent space is nonzero" content: by Nakayama, if `𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪²`
1667	then `𝔪 = 0` (so `spanFinrank 𝔪 = 0`), contradicting the hypothesis. -/
1668	private lemma exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos
1669	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1670	    (h : 0 < (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank) :
1671	    ∃ x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R, x ∉ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := by
1672	  -- By contradiction: assume 𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪², then by Nakayama 𝔪 = 0, but spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1.
1673	  by_contra h_neg
1674	  push Not at h_neg
1675	  have h𝔪_le_sq : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := h_neg
1676	  -- 𝔪² = 𝔪 • 𝔪.
1677	  have hsq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ^ 2
1678	      = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
1679	    rw [sq, ← Ideal.smul_eq_mul]
1680	  have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R).FG :=
1681	    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
1682	  -- Use Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot with N = ⊥.
1683	  have hjac : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ≤ (⊥ : Ideal R).jacobson :=
1684	    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _
1685	  have h_le_smul : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R)
1686	      ≤ ⊥ ⊔ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) •
1687	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
1688	    rw [bot_sup_eq, ← hsq]; exact h𝔪_le_sq
1689	  have h𝔪_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ≤ ⊥ :=
1690	    Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot h𝔪_fg hjac h_le_smul
1691	  have h𝔪_eq_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) = ⊥ := le_bot_iff.mp h𝔪_bot
1692	  have h_span : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0 := by
1693	    rw [h𝔪_eq_bot]; exact Submodule.spanFinrank_bot
1694	  omega

1698	For a Noetherian local ring `R` with `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0`, the

1704	private lemma isDomain_of_isLocalRing_of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_zero
1705	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1706	    (h : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0) : IsDomain R := by

1712	    IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr h𝔪_bot

1821	/-! ### Helper iter-191 Lane G (axiom-clean): zero-divisor witness from a minimal prime
1822	
1823	For a commutative ring `R` and a minimal prime `𝔭 ∈ minimalPrimes R`, every
1824	element of `𝔭` is a zero-divisor in `R`. Concretely: for any `x ∈ 𝔭`, there
1825	exists `y ∈ R, y ≠ 0` with `x * y = 0`.
1826	
1827	Proof: minimal primes are disjoint from non-zero-divisors via
1828	`Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes`, so `x ∈ 𝔭` ⟹
1829	`x ∉ nonZeroDivisors R` ⟹ `∃ y ≠ 0, x * y = 0`.
1830	
1831	This packages the Stacks-00NQ-relevant first step for the `x ∈ 𝔭` case of
1832	`isDomain_of_regularLocal`: when `(x)` is a minimal prime, `x` is a zero-divisor,
1833	so the obstruction reduces to derivation of a contradiction from the
1834	zero-divisor witness (using the regular-local structure of `R` and `R/(x)`). -/
1835	private lemma exists_ne_zero_mul_eq_zero_of_mem_minimalPrimes
1836	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {𝔭 : Ideal R} (h𝔭 : 𝔭 ∈ minimalPrimes R)

1838	    ∃ y : R, y ≠ 0 ∧ x * y = 0 := by
1839	  have hdisj : Disjoint (𝔭 : Set R) (nonZeroDivisors R : Set R) :=
1840	    Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes h𝔭
1841	  have hxNot : x ∉ nonZeroDivisors R := fun hxNZD =>
1842	    (Set.disjoint_left.mp hdisj) hx hxNZD
1843	  have hExistsZD : ¬ ∀ z, x * z = 0 → z = 0 := fun h => hxNot <| by
1844	    rw [mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff]
1845	    refine ⟨h, fun z hz => h z (by rw [mul_comm]; exact hz)⟩
1846	  push Not at hExistsZD
1847	  obtain ⟨y, hxy, hy⟩ := hExistsZD
1848	  exact ⟨y, hy, hxy⟩

1861	`succ` arm without requiring `IsDomain R` (which is the goal we are

2092	private lemma isDomain_of_regularLocal
2093	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2094	    [IsRegularLocalRing R] : IsDomain R := by
2095	  -- Strong induction on `spanFinrank 𝔪`, generalising `R` so the IH applies
2096	  -- to the quotient `R/(x)` at smaller dim.
2097	  suffices haux : ∀ (n : ℕ) (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2098	      [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R],
2099	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = n → IsDomain R by
2100	    exact haux _ R rfl
2101	  intro n
2102	  induction n with
2103	  | zero =>
2104	    intros R _ _ _ _ hdim
2105	    exact isDomain_of_isLocalRing_of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_zero hdim
2106	  | succ k ih =>
2107	    intros R _ _ _ _ hdim
2108	    -- Step 1: pick `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` via Nakayama.
2109	    have hpos : 0 < (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank := by omega
2110	    obtain ⟨x, hxMem, hxNotSq⟩ := exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos hpos
2111	    -- Step 2: instances + IsRegularLocalRing on R/(x) via the axiom-clean helper.
2112	    obtain ⟨hNT, hLR, hRLR, hdim_quot⟩ :=
2113	      regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq hdim x hxMem hxNotSq
2114	    -- Step 3: IH on R/(x) at spanFinrank = k.
2115	    have hDomain_R' : IsDomain (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
2116	      ih (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) hdim_quot
2117	    -- Step 4: (x) is prime in R (R/(x) is a domain).
2118	    haveI hPrime_x : (Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)).IsPrime :=
2119	      (Ideal.Quotient.isDomain_iff_prime _).mp hDomain_R'
2120	    -- Step 5: pick minimal prime 𝔭 ≤ (x).
2121	    obtain ⟨𝔭, h𝔭_min, h𝔭_le⟩ := Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le
2122	      (I := (⊥ : Ideal R)) (J := Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) bot_le
2123	    haveI h𝔭_prime : 𝔭.IsPrime := Ideal.minimalPrimes_isPrime h𝔭_min
2124	    -- Step 6: case split on x ∈ 𝔭 vs x ∉ 𝔭.
2125	    by_cases hxIn : x ∈ 𝔭
2126	    · -- Case `x ∈ 𝔭`: then `𝔭 = (x)` is a minimal prime of `R`. By Helper C
2127	      -- (`notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ`), this is impossible
2128	      -- — the substantive Stacks 00NQ remnant is now packaged in that
2129	      -- helper, with its narrow typed sorry the only remaining gap.
2130	      have h𝔭_eq : 𝔭 = Ideal.span ({x} : Set R) := by
2131	        apply le_antisymm h𝔭_le
2132	        rw [Ideal.span_le, Set.singleton_subset_iff]
2133	        exact hxIn
2134	      have hmin : Ideal.span ({x} : Set R) ∈ minimalPrimes R := h𝔭_eq ▸ h𝔭_min
2135	      exact absurd hmin
2136	        (notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ R hdim x hxMem hxNotSq
2137	          (fun R' _ _ _ _ h => ih R' h))
2138	    · -- Case `x ∉ 𝔭`: `𝔭 ⊆ 𝔪·𝔭` by the `y = x·z, z ∈ 𝔭` argument; Nakayama
2139	      -- (`Submodule.FG.eq_bot_of_le_jacobson_smul`) gives `𝔭 = ⊥`, so `(0)`
2140	      -- is a prime ideal of `R`, hence `IsDomain R`.
2141	      have h𝔭_sub_smul : (𝔭 : Submodule R R) ≤
2142	          Ring.jacobson R • (𝔭 : Submodule R R) := by
2143	        intro y hy
2144	        -- y ∈ 𝔭 ≤ (x), so x | y, so y = x*z for some z.
2145	        have hy_in_x : y ∈ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R) := h𝔭_le hy
2146	        rw [Ideal.mem_span_singleton] at hy_in_x
2147	        obtain ⟨z, rfl⟩ := hy_in_x
2148	        -- Goal: x * z ∈ jacobson R • 𝔭. We have y = x * z ∈ 𝔭 prime, x ∉ 𝔭,
2149	        -- so z ∈ 𝔭.
2150	        have hz_in : z ∈ 𝔭 := by
2151	          rcases h𝔭_prime.mem_or_mem hy with hx_in | hz_in

2305	/-- **Lane G inductive step (iter-185 structural scaffold).** Packages the
2306	inductive step of Stacks 00OD: given a regular local ring `R` of dimension
2307	`k + 1`, plus the inductive hypothesis at dimension `k` (universally
2308	quantified in the ring), produce a regular sequence of length `k + 1` in
2309	the maximal ideal of `R`.
2310	
2311	The body is **axiom-clean** modulo the typed-`sorry` substrate helper
2312	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`. Assembly path:
2313	
2314	1. Helper extracts `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular R x` AND `IsRegularLocalRing
2315	   (R⧸(x))` of `spanFinrank = k`.
2316	2. IH applied on `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` produces a regular sequence
2317	   `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` of length `k` in the maximal ideal there.
2318	3. Lift `rs'_q` to `rs : List R` via `Function.surjInv` of
2319	   `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the section property gives
2320	   `rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) = rs'_q`.
2321	4. Members of `rs` lie in `𝔪` because the maximal ideal of `R⧸(x)` is the
2322	   image of `𝔪` (`IsLocalRing.le_maximalIdeal` applied to the comap chain).
2323	5. Cons via `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons'` to form the length-`(k+1)`
2324	   sequence `x :: rs`.
2325	
2326	This factoring isolates the Mathlib gap to one narrow typed `sorry` instead of
2327	spreading it across the lemma body; future iterations can attack Stacks 00NQ
2328	+ 00NU directly. -/
2329	private lemma regularLocal_inductive_step {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2330	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ℕ}
2331	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
2332	    (IH : ∀ (R' : Type u) [CommRing R'] [IsLocalRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R']
2333	            [IsRegularLocalRing R'],
2334	            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').spanFinrank = k →
2335	            ∃ rs : List R', rs.length = k ∧
2336	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') ∧
2337	              RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs) :
2338	    ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = k + 1 ∧
2339	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
2340	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
2341	  -- Step 1: extract `x ∈ 𝔪` regular on `R` with `R ⧸ (x)` regular local of
2342	  -- `spanFinrank = k`.  This is the consolidated Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate.
2343	  obtain ⟨x, hxMem, hxReg, hRLR, hdim_quot⟩ :=
2344	    exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ hdim
2345	  -- Step 2: apply IH on `R ⧸ (x)` — this gives a regular sequence of length
2346	  -- `k` on the quotient, valued in its maximal ideal.
2347	  obtain ⟨rs'_q, hlen_q, hmem_q, hreg_q⟩ := IH (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) hdim_quot
2348	  -- Step 3: lift `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` to `rs' : List R` via the right
2349	  -- inverse of the (surjective) quotient ring hom.
2350	  let mkq : R →+* R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
2351	  let g : R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} → R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2352	  have hg : ∀ y, mkq (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
2353	  let rs' : List R := rs'_q.map g
2354	  have hlen_rs' : rs'.length = k := by simp [rs', hlen_q]
2355	  have hmkmap : rs'.map mkq = rs'_q := by
2356	    change (rs'_q.map g).map mkq = rs'_q
2357	    rw [List.map_map]
2358	    conv_rhs => rw [← List.map_id rs'_q]
2359	    exact List.map_congr_left fun y _ => hg y
2360	  -- Step 4: each element of `rs'` lies in `𝔪 R` via the comap of `𝔪 (R⧸(x))`.
2361	  -- The maximal ideal of `R ⧸ (x)` comaps back to `𝔪 R` (it's the *unique*
2362	  -- maximal ideal of `R` containing `Ideal.span {x} ⊆ 𝔪`).

2364	    intro r hr
2365	    simp only [rs', List.mem_map] at hr
2366	    obtain ⟨y, hy_mem, rfl⟩ := hr
2367	    -- The comap of `𝔪 (R⧸(x))` under the surjective `mkq` is a maximal ideal
2368	    -- of `R`; since `R` is local, it equals `𝔪 R`.  Hence `g y ∈ comap mkq 𝔪'`
2369	    -- iff `g y ∈ 𝔪 R`.

2371	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))).IsMaximal :=
2372	      Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2373	    have heq : Ideal.comap mkq
2374	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
2375	        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R :=
2376	      (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax_comap
2377	    rw [← heq, Ideal.mem_comap]
2378	    exact (hg y).symm ▸ hmem_q y hy_mem
2379	  -- Step 5: cons `x` onto `rs'` to form the length-`(k+1)` regular sequence.
2380	  refine ⟨x :: rs', ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
2381	  · simp [hlen_rs']
2382	  · intro r hr
2383	    rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr_in
2384	    · exact hxMem
2385	    · exact hmem_rs' r hr_in
2386	  · -- `IsRegular R (x :: rs')` via `IsRegular.cons'`.
2387	    -- Need `IsSMulRegular R x` (have `hxReg`) AND
2388	    -- `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) (rs'.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {x})))`.
2389	    refine RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons' hxReg ?_
2390	    -- After `cons'`: goal is `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) (rs'.map mkq)`
2391	    -- = `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) rs'_q` (by `hmkmap`), implicit ring
2392	    -- `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` (inferred from list type).
2393	    rw [hmkmap]
2394	    -- Goal: `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) rs'_q` (implicit ring `R ⧸ (x)`).
2395	    -- IH provides: `IsRegular (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) rs'_q` (same implicit
2396	    -- ring, but M differs: `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` vs `QuotSMulTop x R = R ⧸ (x • ⊤)`).
2397	    --
2398	    -- **iter-185 typed `sorry` — TECHNICAL BRIDGE (NOT a Mathlib substrate gap).**
2399	    -- The two M's are *equal as sets* — both are the quotient of `R` by the
2400	    -- principal ideal `(x)`, written two different ways:
2401	    --   * `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` — quotient by `Ideal.span {x}` as a Submodule R R;
2402	    --   * `QuotSMulTop x R = R ⧸ (x • ⊤)` — quotient by `x • ⊤` as a Submodule R R.

2406	    -- bridge between the two M's is an `R⧸(x)`-linear equivalence; future iter
2407	    -- can construct this in ~10-20 LOC via:
2408	    --   (a) `Submodule.quotEquivOfEq` from the submodule equality (gives R-linear);
2409	    --   (b) upgrade to `R⧸(x)`-linear via `QuotSMulTop.mem_annihilator`

2411	    --       factors through `R⧸(x)`, and any R-linear equiv between two such

2413	    --   (c) close via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` on this `R⧸(x)`-linear equiv.
2414	    -- This bridge has no substrate dependencies — it's pure bookkeeping.
2415	    --
2416	    -- iter-186 Lane G: closed kernel-clean via the explicit `R⧸(x)`-linear
2417	    -- equiv between the two quotients, then `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`
2418	    -- transports `hreg_q` across.  The two `mapQ` halves use `LinearMap.id`
2419	    -- with `heq.le` / `heq.ge`, and `map_smul'` reduces to `rfl` after

2422	    open scoped Pointwise in
2423	    have heq : (x • (⊤ : Submodule R R)) = (Ideal.span {x} : Submodule R R) := by
2424	      ext y
2425	      simp [Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists, Ideal.mem_span_singleton,
2426	        eq_comm, Dvd.dvd]
2427	    let e : (R ⧸ (x • (⊤ : Submodule R R))) ≃ₗ[R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}]
2428	        (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) := {
2429	      toFun := Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.le
2430	      invFun := Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.ge
2431	      left_inv := by rintro ⟨r⟩; rfl
2432	      right_inv := by rintro ⟨r⟩; rfl
2433	      map_add' := map_add _
2434	      map_smul' := by
2435	        rintro q ⟨r⟩
2436	        induction q using Quotient.inductionOn with
2437	        | _ s => rfl
2438	    }
2439	    exact (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e.symm rs'_q).mp hreg_q

2441	lemma exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal
2442	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2443	    [IsRegularLocalRing R] :
2444	    ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank
2445	        ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
2446	        ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
2447	  -- Strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`, generalising `R` so the inductive
2448	  -- hypothesis can be applied to the quotient `R/(x)` at smaller dimension.
2449	  suffices haux : ∀ (n : ℕ) (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2450	      [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R],
2451	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = n →
2452	      ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧
2453	        (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
2454	        RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs by
2455	    exact haux _ R rfl
2456	  intro n
2457	  induction n with
2458	  | zero =>
2459	    -- Base case `dim 0`: spanFinrank = 0, hence `maximalIdeal R = ⊥` (so `R` is
2460	    -- a field). The empty list is trivially `R`-regular on the nonzero ring `R`.
2461	    intros R _ _ _ _ _hdim
2462	    refine ⟨[], rfl, by simp, ?_⟩
2463	    exact RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil R R
2464	  | succ k ih =>
2465	    -- Inductive case `dim (k + 1)`: delegate to `regularLocal_inductive_step`,
2466	    -- supplying the inductive hypothesis at dimension `k`. The helper handles
2467	    -- the substantive NZD-extraction + quotient-regularity + cons assembly.
2468	    intros R _ _ _ _ hdim
2469	    exact regularLocal_inductive_step (k := k) hdim (fun R' _ _ _ _ h => ih R' h)

2471	/-- **Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay.** Every regular Noetherian
2472	local ring is Cohen–Macaulay: a minimal generating set of `𝔪` is an
2473	`R`-regular sequence of length `dim R`, so `depth(R) ≥ dim R`; combined
2474	with the standard upper bound `depth(R) ≤ dim R` (Stacks 00LK) this gives
2475	`depth(R) = dim R`.
2476	
2477	This is the consumer-facing input for **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
2478	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective
2479	surface): the local ring `O_{S,x}` of a regular projective surface at a
2480	closed point is regular of Krull dimension `2`, hence Cohen–Macaulay,
2481	hence has depth `2`, which is exactly the input the local-cohomology
2482	vanishing `H^i_x(O_S) = 0` for `i < 2` needs (Stacks 0AVF; see Hartshorne
2483	III.7).
2484	
2485	iter-181 Lane G **STRUCTURAL**: body is now decomposed into two
2486	typed helper lemmas — `length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular` (the upper
2487	bound, closed kernel-clean from
2488	`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) and
2489	`exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (the lower bound, typed `sorry` on the
2490	Mathlib gap `IsRegularLocalRing ⟹ IsDomain` + regular-quotient induction).
2491	The combined assembly into `depth = ringKrullDim` is closed inline below,
2492	so the only residual `sorry` in this `instance` body is the named helper. -/
2493	instance of_regular (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2494	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] : CohenMacaulay R where
2495	  depth_eq_krullDim := by
2496	    -- Step 1: simplify `Module.depth` via the `else` branch
2497	    --   (since `𝔪 • ⊤ = 𝔪 ≠ ⊤` for a local ring's maximal ideal).
2498	    have h𝔪 : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R R)
2499	        ≠ (⊤ : Submodule R R) := by
2500	      have heq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R R)
2501	          = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by simp
2502	      rw [heq]
2503	      exact (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal.isMaximal R).ne_top
2504	    rw [Module.depth, if_neg h𝔪]
2505	    -- Step 2: convert RHS to the spanFinrank using
2506	    -- `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`.
2507	    rw [← IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal]
2508	    -- Goal: ((sSup {n | …} : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞)
2509	    --         = ((spanFinrank 𝔪 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)
2510	    -- Step 3: it suffices to show the sSup equals spanFinrank as ℕ∞,
2511	    -- via antisymmetry: upper bound from Helper 1, lower bound from Helper 2.
2512	    have h1 : (sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
2513	        (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
2514	          ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs }
2515	        : ℕ∞) = ((IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank : ℕ∞) := by
2516	      apply le_antisymm
2517	      · -- Upper bound: every element of the sSup-set is at most spanFinrank.
2518	        apply sSup_le
2519	        rintro n ⟨rs, rfl, _, hreg⟩
2520	        have hub := length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular hreg
2521	        rw [← IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal] at hub
2522	        exact_mod_cast hub
2523	      · -- Lower bound: spanFinrank is achieved by Helper 2's regular sequence.
2524	        obtain ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩ := exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal R
2525	        apply le_sSup
2526	        refine ⟨rs, ?_, hmem, hreg⟩
2527	        exact_mod_cast hlen
2528	    rw [h1]
2529	    -- Final coercion: `((n : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) = ((n : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)`
2530	    -- is the standard `Nat.cast`-tower commutation.
2531	    rfl
2532	
2533	end CohenMacaulay
2534	
2535	end RingTheory

1	\chapter{Auslander--Buchsbaum}
2	\label{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.4.b sub-row of Route A
6	% (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the corollary ``regular local ring
8	% \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay'' that A.4.a (the codim-1 extension proof for
9	% Albanese morphisms out of a regular projective surface) consumes. Per
10	% STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
11	% startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the
12	% Albanese geometry and lives entirely under \texttt{RingTheory.*}. The
13	% target Lean file \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean}
14	% does not yet exist; this chapter is the prover-ready specification for
15	% creating it. Note that Mathlib already houses parts of the depth /
16	% projective-dimension API
17	% (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Depth.*},
18	% \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.RegularSequence},
19	% \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.ProjectiveResolution}); a Plan-Agent audit
20	% at the pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}) should confirm what is present
21	% before the prover writes the file, since a fully Mathlib-backed
22	% Auslander--Buchsbaum would collapse this chapter to a re-export.
23	
24	\section{Setup and motivation}
25	\label{sec:ab_setup}
26	
27	A regular projective surface \(S\) over a field --- such as \(\mathbb{P}^1 \times \mathbb{P}^1\)
28	or the product \(C \times C\) of a smooth projective curve \(C\) with itself --- has a regular
29	local ring at every (closed) point, of Krull dimension \(2\). To extend a rational map
30	\(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) from \(S\) to an abelian variety across a codimension-\(2\) closed
31	point, one argues that the local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\) at such a point has \emph{depth at
32	least} \(2\), hence a rational function with no codimension-\(1\) poles on a neighbourhood of \(x\)
33	extends across \(x\). The depth \(\geq 2\) inequality is exactly what the
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum formula gives: for a regular local ring \(R\) one has \(\text{pd}_R(R) = 0\),
35	so
36	\[
37	  \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{pd}_R(R) + \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{depth}(R)
38	\]
39	trivially, but combined with the regular-sequence definition of depth and the fact that a
40	regular local ring of dimension \(d\) admits an \(R\)-regular sequence of length \(d\) generating
41	the maximal ideal, one obtains \(\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)\), i.e.\ \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay
42	(Corollary~REF below). This is the input A.4.a needs.
43	
44	This chapter packages:
45	\begin{enumerate}
46	  \item the regular-sequence definition of depth and its \(\text{Ext}\) reformulation
47	    (Definition~REF and Lemma~REF),
48	  \item the projective-dimension invariant of a finitely-generated module
49	    (Definition~REF),
50	  \item the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma
51	    (Lemma~REF),
52	  \item the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula proper
53	    (Theorem~REF), and
54	  \item the corollary regular \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay
55	    (Corollary~REF).
56	\end{enumerate}
57	A short closing paragraph (Section~REF) explains how A.4.a
58	consumes Corollary~REF to push a codim-1 extension across a
59	codim-2 point.
60	
61	\section{Depth of a module over a local ring}
62	\label{sec:ab_depth}
63	
64	\begin{definition}
65	
66	\leanok
67	  [Depth of a module]
68	  \label{def:depth}
69	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth}
70	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth)
71	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17748-L17759).
72	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
73	  % "Let \(R\) be a ring, and \(I \subset R\) an ideal. Let \(M\) be a finite \(R\)-module.
74	  %  The {\it \(I\)-depth} of \(M\), denoted \(\text{depth}_I(M)\), is defined as follows:
75	  %  \begin{enumerate}
76	  %  \item if \(IM \not = M\), then \(\text{depth}_I(M)\) is the supremum in
77	  %  \(\{0, 1, 2, \ldots, \infty\}\) of the lengths of \(M\)-regular sequences in \(I\),
78	  %  \item if \(IM = M\) we set \(\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty\).
79	  %  \end{enumerate}
80	  %  If \((R, \mathfrak m)\) is local we call \(\text{depth}_{\mathfrak m}(M)\) simply
81	  %  the {\it depth} of \(M\)."
82	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth).}
83	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(I \subseteq R\) be an ideal, and let \(M\) be a finitely
84	  generated \(R\)-module. The \emph{\(I\)-depth} of \(M\), written \(\text{depth}_I(M)\), is the
85	  supremum in \(\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}\) of the lengths of \(M\)-regular sequences contained
86	  in \(I\), provided \(IM \neq M\); if \(IM = M\) we set \(\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty\). When
87	  \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is a Noetherian local ring we abbreviate
88	  \(\text{depth}(M) := \text{depth}_{\mathfrak{m}}(M)\) and call this simply the
89	  \emph{depth} of \(M\). The depth of the ring \(R\) itself is \(\text{depth}(R)\), the depth
90	  of \(R\) as a module over itself.
91	\end{definition}
92	
93	Recall that a sequence \(f_1, \dots, f_r \in R\) is called \emph{\(M\)-regular} if
94	\(M/(f_1, \dots, f_r)M \neq 0\) and for each \(i\), the element \(f_i\) is a non-zero-divisor on
95	\(M/(f_1, \dots, f_{i-1})M\). The empty sequence is regular on \(M\) iff \(M \neq 0\) (it is
96	\emph{not} regular on the zero module, but the convention \(\text{depth}(0) = \infty\)
97	above is the usual one).
98	
99	The two practical computations of depth are the regular-sequence definition (used to
100	establish a \emph{lower} bound) and the \(\text{Ext}\) characterisation (used to establish an
101	\emph{upper} bound and to prove the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequality).
102	
103	\begin{lemma}
104	
105	\leanok
106	  [Depth via \(\text{Ext}\)]
107	  \label{lem:depth_via_ext}
108	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index}
109	  
110	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext)
111	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17845-L17885).
112	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
113	  % "Let \(R\) be a Noetherian local ring with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\).
114	  %  Let \(M\) be a nonzero finite \(R\)-module. Then \(\text{depth}(M)\)
115	  %  is equal to the smallest integer \(i\) such that
116	  %  \(\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)\) is nonzero."
117	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext).}
118	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring with residue field
119	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak{m}\) and let \(M\) be a nonzero finitely generated \(R\)-module. Then
120	  \[
121	    \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \inf\bigl\{\, i \in \mathbb{N} \;\bigm|\; \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \,\bigr\}.
122	  \]
123	\end{lemma}
124	
125	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
126	% "Let \(\delta(M)\) denote the depth of \(M\) and let \(i(M)\) denote
127	%  the smallest integer \(i\) such that \(\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)\)
128	%  is nonzero. We will see in a moment that \(i(M) < \infty\).
129	%  By Lemma REF we have
130	%  \(\delta(M) = 0\) if and only if \(i(M) = 0\), because
131	%  \(\mathfrak m \in \text{Ass}(M)\) exactly means
132	%  that \(i(M) = 0\). Hence if \(\delta(M)\) or \(i(M)\) is \(> 0\), then we may
133	%  choose \(x \in \mathfrak m\) such that (a) \(x\) is a nonzerodivisor
134	%  on \(M\), and (b) \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta(M) - 1\).
135	%  Consider the long exact sequence
136	%  of Ext-groups associated to the short exact sequence
137	%  \(0 \to M \to M \to M/xM \to 0\) by Lemma REF:
138	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
139	%  Since \(x \in \mathfrak m\) all the maps $\Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)
140	%  \to \Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)$ are zero, see
141	%  Lemma REF.
142	%  Thus it is clear that \(i(M/xM) = i(M) - 1\). Induction on
143	%  \(\delta(M)\) finishes the proof."
144	\begin{proof}
145	  
146	  
147	  
148	  
149	\leanok
150	  Write \(\delta := \text{depth}(M)\) and \(i := \inf\{ j : \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \}\).
151	  The base case \(\delta = 0\) is exactly \(\text{Hom}_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0\) (some element of
152	  \(M\) is annihilated by \(\mathfrak{m}\), i.e.\ \(\mathfrak{m} \in \text{Ass}(M)\), i.e.\
153	  \(\mathfrak{m}\) contains a zero-divisor on \(M\)), which is \(i = 0\). For the inductive step
154	  pick \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\) a non-zero-divisor on \(M\) with
155	  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta - 1\). The short exact sequence
156	  \(0 \to M \xrightarrow{x} M \to M/xM \to 0\) induces a long exact sequence of $\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa,
157	  -)\(. Multiplication by \)x\( annihilates each \)\text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)$ (because
158	  \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\) and \(\kappa\) is annihilated by \(\mathfrak{m}\)), so the long exact
159	  sequence breaks into short pieces giving
160	  \(\text{Ext}^{j-1}_R(\kappa, M/xM) \cong \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)\) at the relevant
161	  indices, and the smallest non-vanishing index drops by \(1\). By induction
162	  \(i(M/xM) = i - 1 = \delta - 1\), so \(i = \delta\).
163	\end{proof}
164	
165	\section{Projective dimension}
166	\label{sec:ab_pd}
167	
168	\begin{definition}
169	
170	\leanok
171	  [Projective dimension]
172	  \label{def:projective_dimension}
173	  \lean{Module.projectiveDimension}
174	  % NOTE: Mathlib pinned at b80f227 may already expose this as
175	  % `Module.projectiveDimension` via
176	  % `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.ProjectiveDimension` /
177	  % `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ProjectiveResolution`; the prover should re-export
178	  % rather than re-define if the API is present. This block fixes the
179	  % mathematical content the prover must match.
180	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring and let \(M\) be an \(R\)-module. The \emph{projective
181	  dimension} of \(M\), written \(\text{pd}_R(M)\), is the infimum in
182	  \(\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}\) of the lengths \(n\) of projective resolutions
183	  \[
184	    0 \longrightarrow P_n \longrightarrow P_{n-1} \longrightarrow \cdots
185	    \longrightarrow P_1 \longrightarrow P_0 \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
186	  \]
187	  with each \(P_i\) a projective \(R\)-module. We say \(M\) has \emph{finite projective dimension}
188	  if \(\text{pd}_R(M) < \infty\), and \emph{infinite projective dimension} otherwise. By
189	  convention \(\text{pd}_R(0) = -\infty\) (some references); we use \(\text{pd}_R(0) = 0\) here
190	  (the empty resolution \(0 \to 0\) of length \(0\)), but the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula in
191	  Theorem~REF concerns only nonzero \(M\).
192	  In the local Noetherian setting a finitely generated \(R\)-module of finite projective
193	  dimension admits a \emph{minimal finite free resolution}: a resolution with each \(P_i\)
194	  free of finite rank and each transition map \(P_{i+1} \to P_i\) landing in \(\mathfrak{m} P_i\).
195	\end{definition}
196	
197	The two basic facts about \(\text{pd}_R(M)\) needed downstream are:
198	\begin{itemize}
199	  \item \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) iff \(M\) is projective (in the local Noetherian setting,
200	    iff \(M\) is free);
201	  \item if \(0 \to K \to P \to M \to 0\) is a short exact sequence with \(P\) projective, then
202	    \(\text{pd}_R(M) = \text{pd}_R(K) + 1\) provided \(K \neq 0\).
203	\end{itemize}
204	Both are standard homological algebra and are recorded under
205	\texttt{Module.projectiveDimension\_succ\_iff} or similar in Mathlib.
206	
207	\section{Depth on a short exact sequence}
208	\label{sec:ab_depth_ses}
209	
210	\begin{lemma}
211	
212	\leanok
213	  [Depth on a short exact sequence]
214	  \label{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}
215	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth_of_short_exact}
216	  
217	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses)
218	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17887-L17920).
219	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
220	  % "Let \(R\) be a local Noetherian ring. Let \(0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0\)
221	  %  be a short exact sequence of nonzero finite \(R\)-modules.
222	  %  \begin{enumerate}
223	  %  \item
224	  %  \(\text{depth}(N) \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N'), \text{depth}(N'')\}\)
225	  %  \item
226	  %  \(\text{depth}(N'') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N') - 1\}\)
227	  %  \item
228	  %  \(\text{depth}(N') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N'') + 1\}\)
229	  %  \end{enumerate}"
230	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses).}
231	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring and let
232	  \(0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of nonzero finitely generated
233	  \(R\)-modules. Then
234	  \begin{enumerate}
235	    \item \(\text{depth}(N) \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N'),\; \text{depth}(N'')\bigr)\);
236	    \item \(\text{depth}(N'') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N') - 1\bigr)\);
237	    \item \(\text{depth}(N') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N'') + 1\bigr)\).
238	  \end{enumerate}
239	\end{lemma}
240	
241	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
242	% "Use the characterization of depth using the Ext groups
243	%  \(\Ext^i(\kappa, N)\), see Lemma REF,
244	%  and use the long exact cohomology sequence
245	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
246	%  from Lemma REF."
247	\begin{proof}
248	  
249	  
250	  
251	  
252	\leanok
253	  By Lemma~REF, \(\text{depth}(N')\) (resp.\ \(\text{depth}(N)\),
254	  \(\text{depth}(N'')\)) is the smallest index at which \(\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)\) is
255	  nonzero on \(N'\) (resp.\ \(N\), \(N''\)). Apply the long exact \(\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)\)
256	  sequence to \(0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0\):
257	  \[
258	    \cdots \to \text{Ext}^{i-1}_R(\kappa, N'')
259	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N')
260	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N)
261	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N'')
262	    \to \text{Ext}^{i+1}_R(\kappa, N') \to \cdots
263	  \]
264	  Each of the three inequalities is then a direct read-off of the fact that if two
265	  of the three modules in a length-3 chunk vanish then the middle vanishes (for (1)) /
266	  the boundary terms vanish (for (2),(3)). Compare with the diagonal vanishing argument in
267	  the proof of Lemma~REF.
268	\end{proof}

270	\section{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula}
271	\label{sec:ab_main}
272	
273	\begin{theorem}
274	
275	\leanok
276	  [Auslander--Buchsbaum formula]
277	  \label{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}
278	  \lean{RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula}
279	  
280	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum)
281	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L27207-L27280).
282	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
283	  % "Let \(R\) be a Noetherian local ring. Let \(M\) be a nonzero finite \(R\)-module
284	  %  which has finite projective dimension \(\text{pd}_R(M)\). Then we have
285	  %  \(\)
286	  %  \text{depth}(R) = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)
287	  %  \(\)"
288	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum); cf.\
289	  Matsumura, \emph{Commutative Ring Theory}, Theorem 19.1; Auslander--Buchsbaum,
290	  \emph{Homological dimension in local rings}, 1957.}
291	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring and let \(M\) be a nonzero finitely
292	  generated \(R\)-module of finite projective dimension. Then
293	  \[
294	    \text{pd}_R(M) \;+\; \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \text{depth}(R).
295	  \]
296	\end{theorem}
297

300	%  case is the case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\). In this case, let
301	%  \(\)
302	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
303	%  \(\)
304	%  be a minimal finite free resolution, so \(e = \text{pd}_R(M)\).
305	%  By Lemma REF we may assume all matrix
306	%  coefficients of the maps in the complex are contained in the maximal
307	%  ideal of \(R\). Then on the one hand, by
308	%  Proposition REF we see that
309	%  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq e\). On the other hand, breaking the long
310	%  exact sequence into short exact sequences
311	%  \begin{align*}
312	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e - 1}} \to K_{e - 2} \to 0,\\
313	%  0 \to K_{e - 2} \to R^{n_{e - 2}} \to K_{e - 3} \to 0,\\
314	%  \ldots,\\
315	%  0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
316	%  \end{align*}
317	%  we see, using Lemma REF, that
318	%  \begin{align*}
319	%  \text{depth}(K_{e - 2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1,\\
320	%  \text{depth}(K_{e - 3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2,\\
321	%  \ldots,\\
322	%  \text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e - 1),\\
323	%  \text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e
324	%  \end{align*}
325	%  and since \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\) we conclude \(\text{depth}(R) \leq e\).
326	%  This finishes the proof of the case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\).
327	%

329	%  which is a nonzerodivisor on both \(M\) and \(R\). This is possible, because
330	%  either \(\text{pd}_R(M) > 0\) and \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) by the aforementioned
331	%  Proposition REF or \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) in which
332	%  case \(M\) is finite free hence also \(\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0\).
333	%  Thus \(\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0\) by Lemma REF
334	%  (for example) and we can find an \(x \in \mathfrak m\) which is a nonzerodivisor
335	%  on both \(R\) and \(M\). Let
336	%  \(\)
337	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
338	%  \(\)
339	%  be a minimal resolution as above. An application of the snake lemma
340	%  shows that
341	%  \(\)
342	%  0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to
343	%  M/xM \to 0
344	%  \(\)
345	%  is a minimal resolution too. Thus \(\text{pd}_R(M) = \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\).
346	%  By Lemma REF we have
347	%  \(\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\) and
348	%  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1\).
349	%  Till now depths have all been depths as \(R\) modules, but we observe that
350	%  \(\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\) and similarly for \(R/xR\).
351	%  By induction hypothesis we see that the
352	%  Auslander-Buchsbaum formula holds for \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\). Since the
353	%  depths of both \(R/xR\) and \(M/xM\) have decreased by one and the projective
354	%  dimension has not changed we conclude."
355	\begin{proof}
356	  
357	  
358	\leanok
359	  We induct on \(\text{depth}(M)\).
360	
361	  \emph{Base case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\).} Let \(e := \text{pd}_R(M)\) and pick a minimal
362	  finite free resolution
363	  \[
364	    0 \longrightarrow R^{n_e} \longrightarrow R^{n_{e-1}} \longrightarrow
365	    \cdots \longrightarrow R^{n_0} \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
366	  \]
367	  with all transition maps having matrix coefficients in \(\mathfrak{m}\) (this is the
368	  ``minimal-resolution-after-trimming-trivial-summands'' normalisation, cf.\ Stacks
369	  \texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}). On the one hand, the ``what is exact'' criterion
370	  (Stacks tag 00MF, \texttt{proposition-what-exact}, expressing exactness of a sequence of
371	  finite free modules in terms of depths of ideals of \(r\)-minors) gives
372	  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq e\). On the other hand, splitting the resolution into short exact
373	  sequences
374	  \[
375	    0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to K_{e-2} \to 0, \quad
376	    0 \to K_{e-2} \to R^{n_{e-2}} \to K_{e-3} \to 0, \quad \dots, \quad
377	    0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0,
378	  \]
379	  and applying Lemma~REF iteratively,
380	  yields the chain of inequalities
381	  \(\text{depth}(K_{e-2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1\),
382	  \(\text{depth}(K_{e-3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2\), \dots,
383	  \(\text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e-1)\), and finally
384	  \(\text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e\). Since \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\) we deduce
385	  \(\text{depth}(R) \leq e\). Combined with the previous inequality, $\text{depth}(R) = e =
386	  \text{pd}_R(M) + 0 = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)$.
387	
388	  \emph{Inductive step \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\).} We claim there exists \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\)
389	  which is a non-zero-divisor on both \(R\) and \(M\). Indeed: either \(\text{pd}_R(M) > 0\), in
390	  which case \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) by the ``what is exact'' criterion applied to the start
391	  of a minimal resolution, or \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) in which case \(M\) is finite free and
392	  \(\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0\). Either way both \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) and
393	  \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\), hence \(\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0\) by
394	  Lemma~REF, so a common non-zero-divisor $x \in
395	  \mathfrak{m}\( exists. Take a minimal finite free resolution of \)M$ as above; the snake
396	  lemma applied to multiplication by \(x\) gives a minimal finite free resolution
397	  \[
398	    0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \cdots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to M/xM \to 0
399	  \]
400	  of \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\), of the same length \(e\). So
401	  \(\text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) = \text{pd}_R(M)\). By Stacks
402	  \texttt{lemma-depth-drops-by-one}, both \(\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\) and
403	  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1\), and one verifies
404	  \(\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\) (regular sequences in \(\mathfrak{m}\)
405	  correspond to regular sequences in \(\mathfrak{m}/(x)\)). Apply the inductive hypothesis to
406	  \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\):
407	  \[
408	    \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) + \text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(R/xR).
409	  \]
410	  Substituting yields
411	  \(\text{pd}_R(M) + (\text{depth}(M) - 1) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\), i.e.\
412	  \(\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M) = \text{depth}(R)\), as required.
413	\end{proof}

415	\section{Corollary: regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay}
416	\label{sec:ab_regular_cm}
417	
418	\begin{definition}
419	
420	\leanok
421	  [Cohen--Macaulay local ring]
422	  \label{def:cohen_macaulay_local}
423	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay}
424	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM)
425	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25288-L25299).
426	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
427	  % "A Noetherian local ring \(R\) is called {\it Cohen-Macaulay}
428	  %  if it is Cohen-Macaulay as a module over itself."
429	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM).}
430	  A Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is called \emph{Cohen--Macaulay} if
431	  \(\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)\) (equivalently, if \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay as a module
432	  over itself, where Cohen--Macaulay for a finite \(R\)-module \(M\) means
433	  \(\dim(\text{Supp}(M)) = \text{depth}(M)\), cf.\ Stacks tag 00N3).
434	\end{definition}
435	
436	% NOTE (iter-185 review, supersedes iter-184): the Lean assembly
437	% `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` calls a non-private helper
438	% `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean
439	% L1096 — line drifted from iter-184's L944 reference) that now carries a
440	% structural induction scaffold via `regularLocal_inductive_step`, with the
441	% load-bearing sorry pushed into two named helpers: (i)
442	% `exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` consolidating the
443	% Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate gaps in one signature, and (ii) an inline
444	% technical-bridge sorry inside `regularLocal_inductive_step` for the
445	% `R⧸(x)`-vs-`QuotSMulTop x R` linear-equiv upgrade (~10-20 LOC iter-186 close
446	% via `Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul` + `QuotSMulTop.mem_annihilator`).
447	% Neither has a `\lean{...}` pin in this chapter (the lean-vs-blueprint-checker
448	% iter185-auslander minor flagged adding a pin for the public-facing
449	% `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` itself; iter-186+ chapter touch can add
450	% that hint). The Stacks 00NQ Mathlib gap remains the load-bearing substrate;
451	% iter-186+ work paths unchanged: (a) formalise Stacks 00NQ in project
452	% (~300 LOC); (b) Koszul-homology bypass via `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`;
453	% (c) Mathlib upstream.
454	\begin{corollary}
455	
456	\leanok
457	  [Regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay]
458	  \label{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
459	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular}
460	  
461	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM)
462	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25714-L25730).
463	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
464	  % "Let \(R\) be a regular local ring and let
465	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) be a minimal set of generators
466	  %  for the maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\). Then
467	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) is a regular sequence, and
468	  %  each \(R/(x_1, \ldots, x_c)\) is a regular local ring
469	  %  of dimension \(d - c\). In particular \(R\) is Cohen-Macaulay."
470	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM).}
471	  Every regular Noetherian local ring is Cohen--Macaulay: if \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is
472	  regular local of Krull dimension \(d\), then \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\).
473	\end{corollary}
474	
475	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
476	% "Note that \(R/x_1R\) is a Noetherian local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\)
477	%  by Lemma REF with \(x_2, \ldots, x_d\)
478	%  generating the maximal ideal. Hence it is a regular local ring by definition.
479	%  Since \(R\) is a domain by Lemma REF
480	%  \(x_1\) is a nonzerodivisor."
481	\begin{proof}
482	  
483	  
484	  
485	  
486	\leanok
487	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be regular of dimension \(d\) and pick a minimal generating set
488	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) of \(\mathfrak{m}\). By Stacks tag 00NQ (lemma-regular-domain), \(R\) is
489	  an integral domain, so \(x_1\) is a non-zero-divisor. The quotient \(R/x_1 R\) is a Noetherian
490	  local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\) (by Krull's principal ideal theorem,
491	  \texttt{lemma-one-equation}) with maximal ideal generated by \(x_2, \dots, x_d\), hence
492	  has dimension exactly \(d - 1\) and is again regular of dimension \(d - 1\). Inducting on \(d\),
493	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) is an \(R\)-regular sequence, so
494	  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq d\). The reverse inequality \(\text{depth}(R) \leq \dim(R) = d\)
495	  always holds for any nonzero finite module over a Noetherian local ring
496	  (Stacks tag 00LK, \texttt{lemma-bound-depth}). Thus \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\), i.e.\
497	  \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay.
498	
499	  \emph{Remark on the Auslander--Buchsbaum route.} The same conclusion can be obtained from
500	  Theorem~REF as follows: take \(M := R\) itself. Then \(M\) is
501	  finitely generated and projective (free of rank \(1\)), so \(\text{pd}_R(R) = 0\). The
502	  formula gives \(\text{depth}(R) = 0 + \text{depth}(R)\), a tautology. The non-tautological
503	  use of Auslander--Buchsbaum is rather: for a regular local ring of dimension \(d\), every
504	  finitely generated module \(M\) has \(\text{pd}_R(M) \leq d\) (Stacks tag 00O2,
505	  \texttt{proposition-finite-gl-dim-regular}); applying the formula to such an \(M\) then
506	  relates \(\text{depth}(M)\) to \(d - \text{pd}_R(M)\), which is the form most often used in
507	  practice. The direct regular-sequence argument above is the cleanest proof of
508	  \(R\) Cohen--Macaulay itself.
509	\end{proof}

511	\section{Application: codim-1 extension on a regular surface (gating A.4.a)}
512	\label{sec:ab_application_to_a4a}
513	
514	The downstream consumer is A.4.a: extending a rational map
515	\(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) from a regular projective surface \(S\) to an abelian variety \(A\)
516	across a closed point \(x \in S\) that is missing from the domain of definition \(U \subseteq S\).
517	The classical mechanism (cf.\ Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, III.7) is:
518	
519	\begin{enumerate}
520	  \item The local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\) is a regular Noetherian local ring of Krull
521	    dimension \(2\) (because \(S\) is regular and \(\dim S = 2\)).
522	  \item By Corollary~REF it is Cohen--Macaulay, so
523	    \(\text{depth}(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}) = 2\). Equivalently, the maximal ideal contains an
524	    \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\)-regular sequence of length \(2\) (any two transverse generators).
525	  \item Sections of the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_S\) on \(U = S \setminus \{x\}\) extend
526	    uniquely across \(x\) because \(H^0_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) and
527	    \(H^1_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) (these local-cohomology vanishings are equivalent to depth
528	    \(\geq 2\) at \(x\) by Stacks tag 0AVF; see Hartshorne, III.3.5, for the surface case).
529	  \item For a rational map \(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) with codimension-\(\geq 2\) indeterminacy
530	    locus on a regular surface, the same depth-\(\geq 2\) input produces a sheaf-theoretic
531	    extension of \(f\) to all of \(S\): pull back any sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_A\)-affine-locally,
532	    obtain sections over \(U\), and extend across \(x\) by the above.
533	\end{enumerate}
534	
535	So Corollary~REF is the gating input: the geometric A.4.a
536	proof can be written conditional on the algebraic Auslander--Buchsbaum corollary, and the
537	two halves develop independently --- as long as Theorem~REF and
538	Corollary~REF are formalized in
539	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean} with the signatures pinned
540	in this chapter, A.4.a can consume them as Mathlib-style imports.

23	lemma LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ (M : Type v) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (r : R) :
24	    Function.Exact (r • LinearMap.id : M →ₗ[R] M) (r • (⊤ : Submodule R M)).mkQ := by
25	  intro x
26	  simp [Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists,
27	    Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists]
28	
29	namespace ModuleCat
30	
31	/-- The short (exact) complex `M → M → M⧸xM` obtain from the scalar multiple of `x : R` on `M`. -/
32	@[simps]
33	def smulShortComplex (r : R) :
34	    ShortComplex (ModuleCat R) where
35	  X₁ := M
36	  X₂ := M
37	  X₃ := ModuleCat.of R (QuotSMulTop r M)
38	  f := ModuleCat.ofHom (r • LinearMap.id)
39	  g := ModuleCat.ofHom (r • (⊤ : Submodule R M)).mkQ
40	  zero := by
41	    ext x
42	    exact (LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ M r).apply_apply_eq_zero x
43	
44	lemma smulShortComplex_exact (r : R) : (smulShortComplex M r).Exact := by
45	  simp [smulShortComplex, ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact,
46	    LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ, -LinearMap.coe_smul]
47	
48	instance smulShortComplex_g_epi (r : R) : Epi (smulShortComplex M r).g := by
49	  simpa [smulShortComplex, ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective] using Submodule.mkQ_surjective _
50

/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): depth drops by exactly one for a regular
element (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`).

For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, and `x ∈ 𝔪` an
`M`-regular element, the depth of `M/xM` is exactly one less than the depth of `M`:
`depth(M/xM) + 1 = depth(M)`.

This is one of the four substrate gaps for the Auslander–Buchsbaum n=k+1 step (the
"depth-drops-by-one" piece). The proof packages the long-exact sequence of
`Ext^*(κ, -)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`, using that
`[x]` is zero on every `Ext^*(κ, M)` (since `x ∈ Ann κ`) to break the LES into short
exact pieces `0 → Ext^i(κ, M) → Ext^i(κ, M/xM) → Ext^{i+1}(κ, M) → 0`.

Routed through `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on both sides + the `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`
two-direction bridge that converts a natural-number indexed predicate to a `ℕ∞` inequality. -/
lemma depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] {x : R} (hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
    (hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x) :
    depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1 =
      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
  haveI hMxM_nt : Nontrivial (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
    nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (QuotSMulTop x M) := inferInstance
  -- κ as a ModuleCat for compact notation.
  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
  -- x annihilates κ.
  have hxAnnih : x ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) := by
    have h := Ideal.annihilator_quotient (I := IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
    rw [h]; exact hxMem
  -- The SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`.
  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
    ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
  have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
  have hSf_eq_smul : S.f = x • (𝟙 (ModuleCat.of R M) : _ ⟶ _) := rfl
  -- `[x]_*` is zero on every `Ext^i(κ, M)` via the helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`.
  have hSf_kill : ∀ {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ (ModuleCat.of R M) i),
      e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 := by
    intro i e
    have hcomp :
        e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • e := by
      rw [hSf_eq_smul]
      exact (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk₀ e x).symm
    rw [hcomp]
    exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator e hxAnnih
  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
  · -- (≤): depth M ≤ depth(M/xM) + 1.
    -- For each a : ℕ, `(a : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M → (a : ℕ∞) ≤ depth(M/xM) + 1`.
    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
    · subst h0; exact bot_le
    · obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
      -- From ha : ((m+1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M, get Ext^i(κ, M) = 0 for i ≤ m.
      have hM_vanish : ∀ i : ℕ, i < m + 1 → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 :=
        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (m + 1)).mp ha
      have hcast : ((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (m : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
      rw [hcast]
      -- It suffices to show m ≤ depth(M/xM).
      have hMxM_bound :
          (m : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) := by
        rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
        intro j hj e
        -- Use `covariant_sequence_exact₃` at n₀ = j, n₁ = j + 1.
        have he_extClass : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
          hM_vanish (j + 1) (by omega) _
        obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl he_extClass
        have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := hM_vanish j (by omega) _
        rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
        simpa using hx₂.symm
      gcongr
  · -- (≥): depth(M/xM) + 1 ≤ depth M.
    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
    · subst h0; exact bot_le
    · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
      have hcast : ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
      rw [hcast] at ha
      have hone_ne_top : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤ := by simp
      have hn_le_MxM :
          (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
        (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right hone_ne_top).mp ha
      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j : ℕ, j < n → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
            (ModuleCat.of R (QuotSMulTop x M)) j, e = 0 :=
        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := QuotSMulTop x M) n).mp hn_le_MxM
      rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
      intro i hi e
      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi_pos
      · subst hi0
        -- i = 0 case: x being M-regular ⟹ S.f mono ⟹ postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono.
        haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := by
          rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
          exact hxReg
        have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono κ S.f
        apply hinj
        change e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
          = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ (ModuleCat.of R M) 0).comp
              (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
        rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
        exact hSf_kill e
      · -- i ≥ 1: write i = j + 1, use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
        obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero
          (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi_pos)
        have hj : j < n := by omega
        obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ κ hS e (hSf_kill e) rfl
        have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := hMxM_vanish j hj _
        rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
        simpa using hx₃.symm

end Module

For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the assertion
`1 ≤ depth_𝔪(M)` is equivalent to the existence of an `M`-regular element `x ∈ 𝔪`. This
is a direct consequence of unfolding the depth definition to a regular-sequence supremum
(Nakayama rules out the `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤` trivialisation in the `Nontrivial M` setting) and
reading off a length-1 witness.

This is the substrate companion to `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular`: the
two together implement the Stacks tag 00LE step "depth-drops-by-one for any regular
element" plus its prerequisite "the regular element exists". -/
lemma exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M]
    (h : (1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M) :
    ∃ x : R, x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ∧ IsSMulRegular M x := by
  -- Unfold depth: `𝔪 • ⊤ ≠ ⊤` by Nakayama in the `Nontrivial M` finite setting.
  have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
    Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
  have hdepth_unfold :
      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
        = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
          (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
          ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
    rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
  rw [hdepth_unfold] at h
  have hlt : (0 : ℕ∞) < sSup _ := by
    calc (0 : ℕ∞) < 1 := by norm_num
      _ ≤ _ := h
  obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
  -- rs has positive length (since (0 : ℕ∞) < ↑rs.length).
  have hrs_pos : 0 < rs.length := by
    by_contra h_neg
    push Not at h_neg
    have hk0 : rs.length = 0 := Nat.lt_one_iff.mp h_neg
    rw [hk0] at hrs_len
    simp only [Nat.cast_zero] at hrs_len
    rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
    exact absurd hk_lt (lt_irrefl _)
  rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
  · simp at hrs_pos
  · refine ⟨x, hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self, ?_⟩
    exact ((RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg).1

end Module
