61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
64	
65	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
66	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
67	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
68	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
69	`K(X)`.
70	
71	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
72	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
73	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
74	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
75	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
76	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
77	-/
78	
79	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
80	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
81	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
82	
83	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
84	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
85	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
86	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
87	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
88	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
89	integrality field is needed.
90	
91	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
92	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
93	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
94	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
95	  point : X
96	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
97	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
98	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
99	
100	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition

100	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
101	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
102	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
103	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
104	
105	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
106	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
107	
108	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
109	
110	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
111	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
112	
113	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
114	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
115	
116	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
117	
118	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
119	
120	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
121	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
122	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
123	
124	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
125	
126	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
127	
128	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
129	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
130	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
131	discrete valuation on `f`.
132	
133	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
134	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
135	
136	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
137	
138	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
139	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
140	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
141	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
142	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
143	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
144	
145	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
146	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
147	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
148	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
149	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
150	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
151	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
152	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
153	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
154	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
155	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
156	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
157	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
158	
159	end Scheme.RationalMap
160	
161	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
162	
163	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
164	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
165	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
166	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
167	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
168	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
169	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
170	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
171	
172	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
173	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk
174	`O_{X,Y.point}`. The `[IsIntegral X]` precondition makes
175	`IsDomain (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` available so that the
176	`IsDiscreteValuationRing` predicate is well-formed
177	(`AlgebraicGeometry.instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral`). -/
178	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) [IsIntegral X] : Prop where
179	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk is a discrete valuation
180	  ring. (This is the precise content of Hartshorne's `(*)`; the weaker
181	  `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` derives via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹
182	  `IsPrincipalIdealRing` ⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain via the bridge
183	  instance below.) -/
184	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
185	
186	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
187	synthesis can derive `IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for
188	every prime divisor `Y`. -/
189	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instIsDiscreteValuationRingStalk
190	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
191	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
192	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
193	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
194	
195	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
196	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
197	prime divisor `Y`, via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹ `IsPrincipalIdealRing`
198	⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain (Mathlib's `IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing`
199	+ `IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one`). -/
200	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
201	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
202	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
203	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
204	  haveI : IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
205	    Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
206	  haveI : IsPrincipalIdealRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
207	    IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing
208	  IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one _
209	
210	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
211	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
212	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
213	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.
214	
215	iter-177 status: this packages Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, which Mathlib does not
216	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a
217	nonzero element `f ∈ K(X)^×` of a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely
218	many height-one primes can divide either numerator or denominator); the proof
219	factors through `IsLocallyNoetherian X` + the principal-ideal generation of
220	height-1 primes + the finite irreducible-component decomposition of
221	`V(f₀) ∪ V(f∞)`. The chapter pins this as a separate sub-build deferral
222	(`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5).
223	
224	The statement is generic in `f` (no `f ≠ 0` hypothesis is threaded): on
225	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
226	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
227	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
228	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
229	    (f : X.functionField) :
230	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
231	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by
232	  -- **iter-192 case split + f = 0 branch closed axiom-clean.**
233	  --
234	  -- Case 1 (f = 0): the order function evaluates to
235	  -- `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` at every
236	  -- prime divisor, so the support is empty (finite vacuously).
237	  by_cases hf : f = 0
238	  · subst hf
239	    convert Set.finite_empty
240	    ext Y
241	    simp only [Function.mem_support, ne_eq, Set.mem_empty_iff_false, iff_false,
242	      Decidable.not_not, Scheme.RationalMap.order, map_zero, WithZero.log_zero]
243	  -- Case 2 (f ≠ 0): genuinely needs Hartshorne II.6.1 (Stacks 02RV).
244	  -- The proof for nonzero `f` requires the substrate
245	  -- "for a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely many height-1
246	  -- primes can divide the numerator or denominator of a nonzero rational
247	  -- function" — a Mathlib-pending gap noted in the chapter docstring.
248	  -- Body owed iter-193+; iter-192 closes only the f = 0 vacuous case.
249	  · sorry
250	
251	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
252	
253	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
254	
255	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
256	
257	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
258	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
259	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
260	
261	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
262	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
263	
264	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
265	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
266	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
267	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
268	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
269	
270	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
271	
272	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
273	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
274	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
275	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
276	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
277	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
278	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
279	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
280	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
281	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
282	hypothesised regime. -/
283	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
284	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
285	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
286	
287	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
288	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
289	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
290	multiplicity one. -/
291	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
292	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
293	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
294	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
295	
296	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
297	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
298	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
299	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
300	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
301	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
302	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
303	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
304	
305	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
306	
307	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
308	
309	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
310	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
311	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
312	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
313	
314	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
315	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
316	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
319	
320	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
321	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
322	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
323	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
324	
325	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
326	
327	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
328	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
329	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
330	
331	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
332	
333	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
334	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
335	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
336	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
337	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
338	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
339	
340	@[simp]
341	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
342	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
343	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
344	
345	/-- **Degree of the zero divisor is zero.** A direct consequence of
346	`degree_hom` being an `AddMonoidHom`. -/
347	@[simp]
348	lemma degree_zero : degree (0 : X.WeilDivisor) = 0 := by
349	  rw [← degree_hom_apply]; exact map_zero _
350	
351	/-- **Degree is additive.** A direct consequence of `degree_hom` being an
352	`AddMonoidHom`. -/
353	lemma degree_add (D₁ D₂ : X.WeilDivisor) :
354	    degree (D₁ + D₂) = degree D₁ + degree D₂ := by
355	  rw [← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply]
356	  exact map_add _ _ _
357	
358	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
359	
360	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
361	
362	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
363	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
364	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
365	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
366	
367	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
368	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
369	
370	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
371	following definition, p. 131).
372	
373	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
374	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
375	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
376	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
377	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
378	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
379	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
380	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
381	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
382	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
383	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
384	
385	/-- **The coefficient of `principal f hf` at a prime divisor `Y` is the order of
386	`f` along `Y`.** This is the basic structural unfolding of the
387	`Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` packaging in `principal`; one-line via
388	`Finsupp.ofSupportFinite_coe` from `Mathlib.Data.Finsupp.Defs`.
389	
390	iter-193 substrate helper for the Lane I body close
391	(`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`). -/
392	lemma principal_apply [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
393	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0)
394	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) :
395	    (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y =
396	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f := by
397	  change (Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
398	      (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
399	      (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)) Y = _
400	  rw [Finsupp.ofSupportFinite_coe]
401	
402	/-- **`Scheme.RationalMap.order Y 1 = 0`** — the order of the constant
403	function `1` is `0` at every prime divisor. Direct from
404	`map_one` of `Ring.ordFrac` + `WithZero.log_one`.
405	
406	iter-193 substrate helper for further structural results
407	(`principal_one_eq_zero` and similar). -/
408	@[simp]
409	lemma _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_one
410	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
411	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
412	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)] :
413	    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (1 : X.functionField) = 0 := by
414	  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
415	  rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
416	
417	/-- **The principal divisor of the constant function `1` is the zero
418	divisor.** A direct consequence of `Scheme.RationalMap.order_one`. -/
419	@[simp]
420	lemma principal_one [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
421	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
422	    principal (1 : X.functionField) one_ne_zero = 0 := by
423	  change (_ : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) = (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
424	  apply Finsupp.ext
425	  intro Y
426	  rw [principal_apply]
427	  exact Scheme.RationalMap.order_one Y
428	
429	/-- **The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism `K(X)^× → Div(X)`.**
430	
431	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
432	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`
433	(equivalently: an additive map `(K(X)^×, ·) → (Div(X), +)`).
434	
435	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_hom` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131).
436	
437	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities
438	`v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`. The per-`Y` identities live in
439	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
440	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
441	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
442	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
443	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
444	  toFun u :=
445	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))
446	  map_one' := by
447	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
448	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
449	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),

450	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
451	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
452	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
453	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
454	    congr 1
455	    apply Finsupp.ext
456	    intro Y
457	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
458	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
459	    rw [Units.val_one]
460	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
461	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
462	  map_mul' u v := by
463	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
464	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
465	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
466	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
467	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
468	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
469	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)
470	    -- and `WithZero.log_mul` (the nonzero hypotheses come from
471	    -- `Units.ne_zero` and `map_ne_zero`).
472	    rw [← ofAdd_add]
473	    congr 1
474	    apply Finsupp.ext
475	    intro Y
476	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((↑(u * v) : X.functionField))
477	      = Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑u : X.functionField)
478	      + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑v : X.functionField)
479	    rw [Units.val_mul]
480	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
481	    rw [map_mul]
482	    exact WithZero.log_mul
483	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero u))
484	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero v))
485	
486	/-- **Principal divisors on a complete nonsingular curve have degree zero**
487	(Hartshorne Corollary II.6.10, Stacks 0BE3).
488	
489	For every nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×` on a smooth proper curve `C`
490	over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
491	`deg(div(f)) = 0 ∈ ℤ`.
492	
493	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_deg_zero` (Hartshorne II.6 Cor. 6.10 p. 138).
494	
495	The proof (Hartshorne 6.10): if `f ∈ k̄` is constant then `div(f) = 0` and the
496	claim is trivial. Otherwise the inclusion `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` exhibits `K(C)` as a
497	finite extension of `k̄(f) ≅ k̄(t)`, so the corresponding morphism
498	`φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` is finite, `div(f) = φ^*([0] - [∞])`, and pullback along a
499	finite morphism multiplies degree by `deg(φ)`. Two auxiliary sub-lemmas
500	(finite morphism induced by a non-constant rational function; multiplicativity
501	of degree under finite pullback, Hartshorne II.6.9) are deferred to follow-up
502	iters of `RR.1`. -/
503	theorem principal_degree_zero {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
504	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
505	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
506	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
507	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
508	    (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) :
509	    degree (principal f hf) = 0 := by
510	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10 (iter-178 partial). Case-split on whether every
511	  -- prime-divisor order of `f` vanishes. On a complete nonsingular curve,
512	  -- this case split exactly recovers Hartshorne's constant-vs-non-constant
513	  -- split: `(∀ Y, ord_Y f = 0)` ⟺ `f ∈ k̄ \ {0}` (a nowhere-vanishing
514	  -- rational function on a complete curve is constant; that direction is
515	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10's "if `f ∈ k̄` then `(f) = 0`").
516	  by_cases hconst : ∀ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
517	  · -- Constant branch: every order vanishes ⟹ `principal f hf = 0` ⟹
518	    -- `degree (principal f hf) = degree 0 = 0`.
519	    have hprincipal_zero : principal f hf = 0 := by
520	      apply Finsupp.ext
521	      intro Y
522	      change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
523	      exact hconst Y
524	    rw [hprincipal_zero]
525	    unfold degree
526	    exact Finsupp.sum_zero_index
527	  · -- Non-constant branch (Hartshorne II.6.10 `f ∉ k̄`): the inclusion
528	    -- `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` defines a finite morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the
529	    -- function-field-determines-curve correspondence (Hartshorne I.6.12),
530	    -- and `principal f hf = φ^*([0] - [∞])`. Pullback along a finite
531	    -- morphism of curves multiplies degrees by `deg(φ)` (Hartshorne
532	    -- II.6.9), so `deg(principal f hf) = deg(φ) · deg([0] - [∞]) =
533	    -- deg(φ) · 0 = 0`.
534	    -- This branch is gated on (i) the `φ : C → ℙ¹` construction (Lane 5
535	    -- `RationalCurveIso.lean:morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`, iter-178+),
536	    -- and (ii) the degree-multiplicativity-under-finite-pullback bridge
537	    -- (Hartshorne II.6.9, Mathlib gap).
538	    sorry
539	
540	/-! ## §6. Positive part of a Weil divisor
541	
542	Iter-190 plan-phase Lane I Pin 2 corrective substrate. The carrier
543	`X.WeilDivisor = X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ` is a finitely supported integer-valued
544	function on prime divisors; the *positive part* `(D)_0` is obtained by
545	replacing each coefficient `n_Y` with `max(n_Y, 0)`. Equivalently this is
546	the lattice join `D ⊔ 0` in the pointwise lattice structure (the
547	`semilatticeSup` instance on `Finsupp` is noncomputable). The complementary
548	*negative part* `(D)_∞ := (-D)_0` then gives the canonical decomposition
549	`D = (D)_0 - (D)_∞` into a difference of effective divisors.
550	
551	Blueprint reference: `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart` /
552	`lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` of
553	`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §6 (iter-190 plan-phase additions).
554	-/
555	
556	/-- **Positive part of a Weil divisor**.
557	
558	`positivePart D` is the divisor obtained from `D` by replacing each
559	coefficient `n_Y` with `max(n_Y, 0)` (equivalently `D ⊔ 0` in the
560	pointwise lattice structure on `Finsupp`). For `D = div(f)` on a smooth
561	proper curve `C`, this recovers the divisor-of-zeros `(f)_0` — the
562	formal sum of the zeros of `f` with their multiplicities.
563	
564	The complementary `negativePart D := positivePart (-D)` then gives
565	`D = positivePart D - negativePart D` as a difference of effective
566	divisors.
567	
568	Implementation: `Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)`, the
569	generic Mathlib packaging that maps a finsupp's coefficients through a
570	zero-preserving function. (The `D ⊔ 0` lattice form is equivalent but
571	requires the noncomputable `Finsupp.semilatticeSup` synthesis on
572	`PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ`; the explicit `mapRange` form is more transparent
573	to typeclass synthesis.)
574	
575	Blueprint reference: `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart` (project-bespoke;
576	chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §6, Hartshorne II.6.10 phrasing). -/
577	noncomputable def positivePart (D : X.WeilDivisor) : X.WeilDivisor :=
578	  Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp) D
579	
580	/-- The positive part of the zero divisor is zero. -/
581	@[simp]
582	lemma positivePart_zero : positivePart (0 : X.WeilDivisor) = 0 := by
583	  change Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)
584	    (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) = (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
585	  exact Finsupp.mapRange_zero
586	
587	/-- **Degree of positive part as a sum of capped coefficients.** A purely
588	symbolic Mathlib manipulation: unfolding `positivePart`
589	(= `Finsupp.mapRange (max · 0)`) and `degree` (= `Finsupp.sum (fun _ n => n)`)
590	identifies `degree (positivePart D)` with the sum of `max (D Y) 0` over
591	the support of `D`.
592	
593	This is the iter-192 structural-reduction helper consumed by
594	`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` below. The proof is one line
595	via `Finsupp.sum_mapRange_index` from
596	`Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Finsupp.Basic`. -/
597	lemma degree_positivePart_eq_sum_max (D : X.WeilDivisor) :
598	    degree (positivePart D) = D.sum (fun _ n => max n 0) := by
599	  unfold positivePart degree
600	  exact Finsupp.sum_mapRange_index (h := fun _ b => b) (by intro _; rfl)
601	
602	/-- **Positive part of a `Finsupp.single`.** Pointwise extraction of the
603	mapRange definition of `positivePart`: for a one-point-supported Weil divisor
604	`Finsupp.single Y n`, the positive part is `Finsupp.single Y (max n 0)`.
605	A direct consequence of `Finsupp.mapRange_single` (with `max 0 0 = 0` as
606	the zero-preservation witness).
607	
608	iter-193 substrate helper paving the way for the Lane I body close. -/
609	@[simp]
610	lemma positivePart_single (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
611	    positivePart (Finsupp.single Y n : X.WeilDivisor) =
612	      Finsupp.single Y (max n 0) := by
613	  change Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)
614	      (Finsupp.single Y n) = _
615	  rw [Finsupp.mapRange_single]
616	
617	/-- **Degree of a `Finsupp.single` Weil divisor.** The degree of the
618	one-point-supported Weil divisor `Finsupp.single Y n` is `n`. A direct
619	consequence of `Finsupp.sum_single_index`.
620	
621	iter-193 substrate helper for `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`. -/
622	@[simp]
623	lemma degree_single (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
624	    degree (Finsupp.single Y n : X.WeilDivisor) = n := by
625	  unfold degree
626	  change (Finsupp.single Y n : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n) = n
627	  exact Finsupp.sum_single_index rfl
628	
629	/-- **Sum-over-prime-divisors lower bound via a single contributing point.**
630	If `f` has order `1` at some prime divisor `Y₀`, then the degree of the positive
631	part of `principal f hf` is at least `1`.
632	
633	iter-193 substrate helper consumed by `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`:
634	it formalises Step 2 of the Hartshorne II.6.9 recipe (`hlp` produces
635	the local-parameter prime divisor `Y₀` with coefficient `1`). -/
636	lemma one_le_degree_positivePart_principal_of_order_one
637	    [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
638	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
639	    (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) (Y₀ : X.PrimeDivisor)
640	    (h₀ : Scheme.RationalMap.order Y₀ f = 1) :
641	    1 ≤ degree (positivePart (principal f hf)) := by
642	  classical
643	  rw [degree_positivePart_eq_sum_max]
644	  -- The sum is over a finset; isolate the Y₀ contribution.
645	  have hY₀_supp :
646	      Y₀ ∈ (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).support := by
647	    rw [Finsupp.mem_support_iff]
648	    rw [principal_apply]
649	    rw [h₀]
650	    exact one_ne_zero
651	  -- For the sum to be ≥ 1, isolate the Y₀ term (= 1) and the rest (≥ 0).
652	  have h_split :
653	      (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).sum
654	          (fun _ n => max n 0) =
655	        max ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y₀) 0 +
656	          ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).support.erase Y₀).sum
657	            (fun Y => max
658	              ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y) 0) := by
659	    rw [show (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).sum
660	            (fun _ n => max n 0) =
661	          (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).support.sum
662	            (fun Y => max
663	              ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y) 0)
664	        from rfl]
665	    rw [Finset.sum_erase_eq_sub hY₀_supp]
666	    ring
667	  rw [h_split]
668	  -- The Y₀ term equals max 1 0 = 1.
669	  have hY₀_val :
670	      (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y₀ = 1 := by
671	    rw [principal_apply]; exact h₀
672	  rw [hY₀_val]
673	  -- The remainder is a sum of non-negative integers, hence ≥ 0.
674	  have h_nonneg :
675	      0 ≤ ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).support.erase Y₀).sum
676	        (fun Y => max
677	          ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y) 0) :=
678	    Finset.sum_nonneg (fun Y _ => le_max_right _ _)
679	  -- Conclude: 1 = max 1 0 ≤ max 1 0 + 0 ≤ LHS + remainder.
680	  have : (max (1 : ℤ) 0) = 1 := by norm_num
681	  rw [this]
682	  linarith
683	
684	/-- **Generic Finsupp identity**: the sum of clipped non-negative parts equals
685	the sum of the coefficients over the positive-coefficient sub-support.
686	
687	For a finsupp `D : α →₀ ℤ`, `D.sum (max · 0)` agrees with the unclipped sum
688	`∑ D` restricted to the finset `{a ∈ supp D | 0 < D a}`. Negative-coefficient
689	points contribute `0` to the max and drop out; positive-coefficient points
690	contribute `D a` to both sides; the (vacuously zero) `D a = 0` case is excluded
691	by the `supp` filter on both sides.
692	
693	iter-195 Lane I substrate helper for `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`:
694	it formalises the "the positive part of `D` is supported on the positive-coefficient
695	points" identity, which is Step 2.5 of the Hartshorne II.6.9 recipe (between the
696	`degree_positivePart_eq_sum_max` unfolding and the ramification-inertia chase). -/
697	lemma _root_.Finsupp.sum_max_zero_eq_sum_filter_pos {α : Type*}
698	    (D : α →₀ ℤ) :
699	    D.sum (fun _ n => max n 0) =
700	      ∑ a ∈ D.support.filter (fun a => 0 < D a), D a := by
701	  classical
702	  rw [Finsupp.sum, Finset.sum_filter]
703	  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
704	  intro a _
705	  by_cases hpos : 0 < D a
706	  · simp [hpos]
707	    omega
708	  · simp [hpos]
709	    omega

969	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.9 specialised to `D = [∞]`** — typed-sorry pin (body
970	iter-194+).
971	
972	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically
973	closed field `kbar`, equipped with a finite `k̄(ℙ¹)`-algebra structure on
974	`K(C)` (the canonical `φ`-induced one for a non-constant morphism
975	`φ : C → ℙ¹`), and a non-zero element `t ∈ K(ℙ¹)` which is a **uniformiser**
976	on `ℙ¹` (its zero divisor has degree `1`, encoded by `hLPUnif`: a unique
977	prime divisor `Y₀` of `ℙ¹` with `order Y₀ t = 1`, and no other prime
978	divisor has positive order), the degree of the positive part of the
979	principal divisor of `algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) t` equals the function-field
980	extension degree:
981	\[
982	  \deg\bigl((\mathrm{div}\,(\varphi^{\#}\,t))_0\bigr)
983	  \;=\; [K(C) : k̄(\mathbb{P}^1)] \;=\;
984	   \mathrm{Module.finrank}_{k̄(\mathbb{P}^1)} K(C).
985	\]
986	Equivalently, `(div (φ^# t))_0 = φ^*[Y₀]` and `deg(φ^*[Y₀]) = deg(φ)`
987	(Hartshorne II.6.9 specialised at the divisor `[Y₀] ∈ Div(ℙ¹)`).
988

990	the signature now drops the abstract `K` parameter and pins to
991	`(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField`, with the uniformiser
992	hypothesis `hLPUnif` enforcing the local-parameter constraint
993	correctly (iter-193 `hlp` was insufficient; counter-witness
994	`K=K(C), t=u(u-1)` is now excluded because `t` lives strictly in
995	`K(ℙ¹)` and `hLPUnif` requires `t` to have a single zero of order
996	`1` on ℙ¹, ruling out functions with multiple zeros). The body
997	(~50-80 LOC owed iter-194+) chains
998	`Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` + `Ideal.finrank_quotient_map` on
999	the Dedekind extension `A → B` at the maximal ideal
1000	`m_{Y₀} = (t) ⊂ A`, per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md` Decision 2.

1002	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`
1003	(Hartshorne II.6.9, p. 137; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §6). -/
1004	theorem degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank
1005	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1006	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
1007	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
1008	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
1009	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
1010	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
1011	    [IsLocallyNoetherian (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
1012	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
1013	    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
1014	       C.left.functionField]
1015	    [Module.Finite (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
1016	       C.left.functionField]
1017	    (t : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField)
1018	    (halg : algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
1019	       C.left.functionField t ≠ 0)
1020	    (hLPUnif : ∃ Y₀ : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.PrimeDivisor,
1021	       Scheme.RationalMap.order Y₀ t = 1 ∧
1022	       ∀ Y : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.PrimeDivisor,
1023	         Scheme.RationalMap.order Y t > 0 → Y = Y₀) :
1024	    degree (positivePart
1025	      (principal (algebraMap _ C.left.functionField t) halg)) =
1026	      (Module.finrank (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
1027	                       C.left.functionField : ℤ) := by
1028	  -- **Iter-194 refactor v2** (lane-i-localparameter-signature-v2):
1029	  -- the signature now drops the abstract `K` parameter and pins to
1030	  -- `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField`, with the uniformiser
1031	  -- hypothesis `hLPUnif` enforcing the local-parameter constraint
1032	  -- correctly (iter-193 `hlp` was insufficient; counter-witness
1033	  -- `K=K(C), t=u(u-1)` is now excluded because `t` lives strictly in
1034	  -- `K(ℙ¹)` and `hLPUnif` requires `t` to have a single zero of order
1035	  -- 1 on ℙ¹, ruling out functions with multiple zeros).
1036	  --
1037	  -- **Iter-194 partial structural advance.** We unpack the uniformiser

1110	/-! ## §7. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
1111	
1112	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
1113	
1114	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
1115	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
1116	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
1117	
1118	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
1119	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
1120	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
1121	**divisor class group** of `X`.
1122	
1123	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
1124	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
1125	
1126	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
1127	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
1128	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
1129	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf
1130	
1131	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
1132	
1133	end AlgebraicGeometry

256	  Let \(X\) satisfy \((*)\). For a prime divisor \(Y \subset X\) with generic
257	  point \(\eta \in Y\), the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a discrete
258	  valuation ring (by regularity in codimension one) with quotient field the
259	  function field \(K(X)\). For a nonzero rational function \(f \in K(X)^{\times}\)
260	  the \emph{order of \(f\) along \(Y\)} is the integer
261	  \[
262	    \ord_Y(f) \;:=\; v_Y(f) \;\in\; \mathbb Z,
263	  \]
264	  where \(v_Y\) is the normalised discrete valuation associated to
265	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\). If \(\ord_Y(f) > 0\), \(f\) has a \emph{zero of
266	  order \(\ord_Y(f)\) along \(Y\)}; if \(\ord_Y(f) < 0\), \(f\) has a \emph{pole
267	  of order \(-\ord_Y(f)\) along \(Y\)}.

273	  valuation of \(f\) at \(P\).

275	  \texttt{dvr-rationalmap-order}).} The iter-175 Lean signature is
276	  \begin{quote}

278	    \phantom{}\quad[IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)\\
279	    \phantom{}\quad[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]\\

291	  \begin{itemize}
292	    \item \texttt{Ring.ordFrac R : K \(\to\!\!*_0\) \(\mathbb Z^{m_0}\)}

373	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over a field \(k\) and let \(P \in C\) be a

380	  integral closed subscheme of the \(1\)-dimensional integral scheme \(C\).
381	  Conversely, every prime divisor on \(C\) is of this form, so an arbitrary
382	  divisor on \(C\) is a finite formal \(\mathbb Z\)-linear combination
383	  \(\sum n_i [P_i]\) of closed points.

547	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, Lemma~6.1 and the following definition,
548	  p.~131.}
549	  Let \(X\) satisfy \((*)\). For a nonzero rational function \(f \in K(X)^{\times}\),
550	  Hartshorne's Lemma~6.1 asserts that \(\ord_Y(f) = 0\) for all but finitely
551	  many prime divisors \(Y\) on \(X\); hence the formal sum
552	  \[
553	    \mathrm{div}(f) \;:=\; \sum_{\substack{Y \subset X \\ \text{prime divisor}}}
554	    \ord_Y(f) \cdot Y \;\in\; \mathrm{Div}(X)
555	  \]
556	  has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor, called the
557	  \emph{principal divisor of \(f\)}. On a smooth proper curve \(C\) over \(\bar k\)
558	  the same definition specialises to
559	  \(\mathrm{div}(f) = \sum_{P \in C \text{ closed}} \ord_P(f) \cdot [P]\), the formal
560	  sum over closed points.
561	\end{definition}
562	\begin{theorem}
563	\leanok
564	[The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism]
565	  \label{thm:principal_hom}
566	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_hom}
567	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, the paragraph after the principal-divisor
568	  % definition, p.~131 (read from
569	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 148).
570	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Note that if \(f, g \in K^\ast\), then \((f/g) = (f) - (g)\)
571	  % because of the properties of valuations. Therefore sending a function
572	  % \(f\) to its divisor \((f)\) gives a homomorphism of the multiplicative
573	  % group \(K^\ast\) to the additive group \(\operatorname{Div} X\), and the
574	  % image, which consists of the principal divisors, is a subgroup of
575	  % \(\operatorname{Div} X\)."
576	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p.~131 (group-homomorphism remark).}
577	  Let \(X\) satisfy \((*)\). The principal-divisor map
578	  \(\mathrm{div} \colon K(X)^{\times} \to \mathrm{Div}(X)\) is a group homomorphism from the
579	  multiplicative group of nonzero rational functions to the additive group
580	  of Weil divisors. Concretely
581	  \[
582	    \mathrm{div}(fg) \;=\; \mathrm{div}(f) + \mathrm{div}(g),
583	    \qquad
584	    \mathrm{div}(f^{-1}) \;=\; -\mathrm{div}(f),
585	    \qquad
586	    \mathrm{div}(1) \;=\; 0.
587	  \]
588	\end{theorem}
589	\begin{proof}
590	  Each prime divisor \(Y\) on \(X\) contributes a discrete valuation \(v_Y\) on
591	  \(K(X)\) (\ref{def:principal_divisor}); by the standard DVR axioms,
592	  \(v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)\) and \(v_Y(f^{-1}) = -v_Y(f)\) for $f, g \in
593	  K(X)^{\times}\(, and \)v_Y(1) = 0$. Summing these identities over all prime
594	  divisors of \(X\) (a sum that has finite support on each factor by
595	  \ref{thm:principal_hom}, so the support of the sum is the union
596	  of the two finite supports and remains finite) gives the three displayed
597	  identities.
598	\end{proof}
599	\begin{theorem}
600	\leanok
601	[Principal divisors have degree zero on a complete nonsingular curve]
602	  \label{thm:principal_deg_zero}
603	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero}
604	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, Corollary~6.10, p.~138 (read from
605	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 155).
606	  % Stacks pointer: tag 0BE3.
607	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Corollary 6.10. \emph{A principal divisor on a complete
608	  % nonsingular curve \(X\) has degree zero. Consequently the degree function
609	  % induces a surjective homomorphism $\deg \colon \operatorname{Cl} X \to
610	  % \mathbf Z$.}"
611	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, Corollary~6.10, p.~138.}
612	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over an algebraically closed field
613	  \(\bar k\). For every nonzero rational function \(f \in K(C)^{\times}\) the
614	  associated principal divisor has degree zero:
615	  \[
616	    \deg(\mathrm{div}(f)) \;=\; 0 \;\in\; \mathbb Z.
617	  \]
618	\end{theorem}
619	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Let \(f \in K(X)^\ast\). If \(f \in k\), then
620	% \((f) = 0\), so there is nothing to prove. If \(f \notin k\), then the
621	% inclusion of fields \(k(f) \subset K(X)\) induces a finite morphism
622	% \(\varphi \colon X \to \mathbf P^1\). It is a morphism by (I, 6.12). We
623	% have \((f) = \varphi^\ast(\{0\} - \{\infty\})\). Since \(\{0\} - \{\infty\}\)
624	% is a divisor of degree 0 on \(\mathbf P^1\), we conclude that \((f)\) has
625	% degree 0 on \(X\)."
626	\begin{proof}
627	  Following Hartshorne's Corollary~6.10. If \(f \in \bar k \subset K(C)\) is
628	  a constant scalar, then \(f\) has no zeros or poles, so \(\mathrm{div}(f) = 0\) and
629	  \(\deg(\mathrm{div}(f)) = 0\). Otherwise the inclusion of fields
630	  \(\bar k(f) \subset K(C)\) exhibits \(K(C)\) as a finite extension of the
631	  rational function field \(\bar k(f) \cong \bar k(t)\), so the corresponding
632	  morphism \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is finite (by the
633	  smooth-proper-curve / function-field correspondence on which a curve is
634	  determined by its function field, classical I.6.12). The principal
635	  divisor \(\mathrm{div}(f)\) equals the pullback \(\varphi^{*}([0] - [\infty])\)
636	  along \(\varphi\) of the degree-\(0\) divisor \([0] - [\infty]\) on
637	  \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\); since pullback along a finite morphism
638	  multiplies degrees by \(\deg(\varphi)\), we conclude
639	  $\deg(\mathrm{div}(f)) = \deg(\varphi) \cdot \deg([0] - [\infty]) = \deg(\varphi)
640	  \cdot 0 = 0$.
641	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The two auxiliary statements ``the inclusion
642	  \(\bar k(f) \subset K(C)\) defines a finite morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1\)''
643	  and ``pullback along a finite morphism of curves multiplies degrees by
644	  the degree of the morphism'' (Hartshorne's Proposition~II.6.9, the
645	  multiplicativity of degree) are themselves separable sub-lemmas of
646	  \texttt{RR.1}; they are stated here for completeness and slotted to be
647	  closed in a follow-up iteration. The signature
648	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal\_degree\_zero}
649	  is recorded as the public face of this theorem and is depended on by
650	  the \texttt{RR.3}/\texttt{RR.4} chain.
651	\end{proof}
652	\section{Positive part of a Weil divisor}
653	% NOTE (iter-190 plan-phase): added to host the Pin 2 corrective per
654	% `task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.lean.md`
655	% (iter-189). `Hom.poleDivisor` is being refactored to return the
656	% positive part of `principal (φ^* t_∞)` so that
657	% `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` is mathematically correct (the principal
658	% divisor has degree 0 by `thm:principal_deg_zero`; only its
659	% positive part can have positive degree).
660	\begin{definition}
661	
662	
663	\leanok
664	[Positive part of a Weil divisor]
665	  \label{def:WeilDivisor_positivePart}
666	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart}
667	  \textit{Source: Project-bespoke (Hartshorne II.6.10 phrasing).}
668	  Let \(X\) be a scheme satisfying the codim-1 hypotheses of
669	  \ref{def:codim1_cycles}. For a Weil divisor
670	  \(D = \sum_Y n_Y \cdot Y \in \mathrm{Div}(X)\), the \emph{positive part}
671	  \((D)_0 \in \mathrm{Div}(X)\) is the Weil divisor
672	  \[
673	    (D)_0 \;:=\; \sum_{Y \,:\, n_Y > 0} n_Y \cdot Y.
674	  \]
675	  Concretely, \((D)_0\) is obtained from \(D\) by replacing each coefficient
676	  \(n_Y\) with \(\max(n_Y, 0)\). Equivalently, in the \(\Finsupp{\mathrm{PrimeDivisor}(X)}{\mathbb Z}\)
677	  presentation, the positive part is obtained either by the pointwise lattice
678	  join with the zero divisor — \(\,(D)_0 = D \sqcup 0\,\) — or, equivalently
679	  and more transparently for typeclass synthesis, by mapping the coefficients
680	  through the zero-preserving function \(n \mapsto n \sqcup 0\):
681	  \(\,(D)_0 = \mathtt{Finsupp.mapRange}\;(n \mapsto n \sqcup 0)\;D\). The two
682	  forms agree by the universal property of \(\mathtt{Finsupp.mapRange}\). The
683	  Lean implementation uses the \(\mathtt{mapRange}\) form to avoid the
684	  noncomputable \(\Finsupp.\mathtt{semilatticeSup}\) synthesis. The complementary \emph{negative part}
685	  \((D)_\infty\) is defined by \((D)_\infty := (-D)_0\), so that
686	  \(D = (D)_0 - (D)_\infty\) is the canonical decomposition into an
687	  effective (non-negative-coefficient) divisor minus an effective divisor.
688	  On a smooth proper curve \(C\) over \(\bar k\), where the prime divisors
689	  are the closed points and the coefficients are integers, the positive part
690	  reduces to keeping only the closed points whose multiplicity is positive
691	  — i.e. precisely the zeros of any rational function whose principal
692	  divisor is \(D\), in the case \(D = \mathrm{div}(f)\).
693	\end{definition}
694	\begin{lemma}
695	
696	\leanok
697	[Degree of the positive part of \(\mathrm{div}(f)\) for a non-constant rational
698	function equals the function-field extension degree]
699	  \label{lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank}
700	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank}
701	  % NOTE (iter-194 refactor v2 `lane-i-localparameter-signature-v2`):
702	  % the iter-193 `hlp`-augmented signature was diagnosed STILL false by

708	  % uniformiser hypothesis
709	  %   `hLPUnif : \exists Y_0 \in \text{PrimeDivisor}(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}),

input.command:
.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py --provider kimi "I need to prove in Lean 4 (Mathlib) the following claim:

For a Noetherian integral scheme X regular in codimension one, and a nonzero rational function f in K(X)^x, the set { Y : X.PrimeDivisor | ord_Y(f) ≠ 0 } is finite (Hartshorne II.6.1 / Stacks 02RV).

In our Lean encoding:
- X : Scheme with [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
- X.PrimeDivisor is a structure with point : X and coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
- Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f := WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
- Ring.ordFrac : K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰ from Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic

The hypothesis: f ≠ 0 in X.functionField (the function field of X), and we want
  (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite

Question: What is the cleanest Mathlib-based proof strategy? What existing Mathlib bridges (functionField_isFractionRing_of_isAffineOpen, Ideal.finite_minimalPrimes_of_isNoetherianRing, AlgebraicGeometry.exists_isAffineOpen, etc.) are most useful? I want concrete Mathlib lemma names and a structural skeleton.

Note: the answer probably starts by writing f = a/b for a, b in an affine open ring, then bounding non-trivial-order Y's by minimal primes of (a) and (b). I want the Mathlib mechanics — what's actually available?" 2>&1 | head -200

-- order_neg: order Y (-f) = order Y f
example (f : X.functionField) :
    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (-f) = Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f := by
  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
  rw [show ((Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)) (-f)) =
        Ring.ordFrac _ f from by
      rw [show (-f : X.functionField) = (-1) * f from by ring,
          map_mul,
          show (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)) (-1) = 1 from by
            rw [show (-1 : X.functionField) = -((1 : X.functionField)) from by ring]
            have : (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)) (-(1 : X.functionField)) =
                (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)) 1 := by
              -- ord of (-1) should = ord of 1 in a domain
              sorry
            rw [this, map_one],
            one_mul]]

example (u : (X.functionField)ˣ) :
    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((u : X.functionField)⁻¹) =

  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order

    by rw [show ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) =
              (u : X.functionField)⁻¹ from by simp]
       sorry]
  sorry

import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.WeilDivisor
open AlgebraicGeometry
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
  (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]

-- order on inv of unit
lemma test_order_units_inv (u : (X.functionField)ˣ) :
    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) =
      -Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (u : X.functionField) := by
  have hu : (u : X.functionField) ≠ 0 := Units.ne_zero u
  have huinv : ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) ≠ 0 := Units.ne_zero u⁻¹
  have hmul : ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) * (u : X.functionField) = 1 := by
    rw [← Units.val_mul, inv_mul_cancel, Units.val_one]
  have key : Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) * (u : X.functionField)) = 0 := by
    rw [hmul]
    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
  have step : Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) * (u : X.functionField)) =
      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (u : X.functionField) := by
    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
    rw [map_mul]
    exact WithZero.log_mul ((map_ne_zero _).mpr huinv) ((map_ne_zero _).mpr hu)
  linarith [key, step]

    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
    (f : X.functionField) :

/-! ### Order-on-curve algebraic identities

iter-198 §2 substrate lemmas (Lane WD-A4a PUSH-BEYOND). These are
axiom-clean per-prime-divisor algebraic identities on
`Scheme.RationalMap.order` — direct consequences of `Ring.ordFrac` being
a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom composed with `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
(which is additive on nonzero arguments, junk-zero on `0`).

The lemmas:

- `Scheme.RationalMap.order_zero`: `ord_Y 0 = 0` (junk convention).
- `Scheme.RationalMap.order_mul_of_ne_zero`: `ord_Y (f·g) = ord_Y f +
  ord_Y g` when both `f, g ≠ 0`.
- `Scheme.RationalMap.order_units_inv`: `ord_Y u⁻¹ = -ord_Y u` for a
  unit `u : K(X)ˣ`.

Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` §2 (Hartshorne II §6 valuation
identities `v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f)+v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(f⁻¹) = -v_Y(f)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`). -/

/-- `ord_Y 0 = 0` by the junk-on-zero convention.

Direct from `Ring.ordFrac _` being a monoid-with-zero hom (`map_zero`)
and `WithZero.log_zero`. -/
@[simp]
lemma _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_zero
    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)] :
    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (0 : X.functionField) = 0 := by
  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
  rw [map_zero, WithZero.log_zero]

/-- `ord_Y (f · g) = ord_Y f + ord_Y g` when `f, g ≠ 0`.

Direct from `Ring.ordFrac _` being a monoid-with-zero hom (`map_mul`)
and `WithZero.log_mul` (which requires both factors nonzero). -/
lemma _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_mul_of_ne_zero
    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
    {f g : X.functionField} (hf : f ≠ 0) (hg : g ≠ 0) :
    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (f * g) =
      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y g := by
  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
  rw [map_mul]
  exact WithZero.log_mul ((map_ne_zero _).mpr hf) ((map_ne_zero _).mpr hg)

/-- `ord_Y u⁻¹ = -ord_Y u` for a unit `u : K(X)ˣ`.

Combines `order_mul_of_ne_zero` with `order_one` and the identity
`u⁻¹ · u = 1`. -/
lemma _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_units_inv
    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
    (u : (X.functionField)ˣ) :
    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) =
      -Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (u : X.functionField) := by
  have hu : (u : X.functionField) ≠ 0 := Units.ne_zero u
  have huinv : ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) ≠ 0 :=
    Units.ne_zero u⁻¹
  have hmul : ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) *
      (u : X.functionField) = 1 := by
    rw [← Units.val_mul, inv_mul_cancel, Units.val_one]
  have key : Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
      (((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) *
        (u : X.functionField)) = 0 := by
    rw [hmul]
    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
  have step : Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
      (((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) *
        (u : X.functionField)) =
      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
        ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) +
      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (u : X.functionField) :=
    Scheme.RationalMap.order_mul_of_ne_zero Y huinv hu
  linarith [key, step]

370	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
371	
372	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
373	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
374	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
375	
376	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
377	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
378	
379	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
380	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
381	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
382	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
383	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
384	
385	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
386	
387	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
388	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
389	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
390	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
391	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
392	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
393	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
394	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
395	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
396	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
397	hypothesised regime. -/
398	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
399	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
400	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
401	
402	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
403	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
404	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
405	multiplicity one. -/
406	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
407	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
408	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
409	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
410	
411	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
412	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
413	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
414	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
415	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
416	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
417	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
418	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
419

423	
424	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
425	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree

/-- `ord_Y f⁻¹ = -ord_Y f` for any `f : K(X)`.

The `f = 0` case is junk-vacuous (`f⁻¹ = 0` in a `GroupWithZero`, both sides
are zero). For `f ≠ 0` this is the DVR-valuation identity `v(f⁻¹) = -v(f)`.

Direct from `Ring.ordFrac _` being a monoid-with-zero hom (`map_inv₀`)
and `WithZero.log_inv` (which holds for all `WithZero (Multiplicative G)`
elements including zero, by the junk-on-zero convention). -/
lemma _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_inv
    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
    (f : X.functionField) :
    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f⁻¹ = -Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f := by
  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
  rw [map_inv₀, WithZero.log_inv]

/-- `ord_Y u⁻¹ = -ord_Y u` for a unit `u : K(X)ˣ`.

A specialisation of `order_inv` to the unit form
`((u⁻¹ : K(X)ˣ) : K(X)) = (u : K(X))⁻¹`, useful at call sites that thread
`Units` (e.g. the `principal_hom : K(X)ˣ →* Multiplicative Div(X)` body). -/
lemma _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_units_inv
    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
    (u : (X.functionField)ˣ) :
    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) =
      -Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (u : X.functionField) := by
  rw [show ((u⁻¹ : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField) =
        (u : X.functionField)⁻¹ from by simp]
  exact Scheme.RationalMap.order_inv Y _

# AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean — iter-198 prover report

## Summary

- **Declarations added (6, all axiom-clean)**:
  - `Scheme.RationalMap.order_zero` (L233, §2 substrate)
  - `Scheme.RationalMap.order_mul_of_ne_zero` (L242, §2 substrate)
  - `Scheme.RationalMap.order_inv` (L258, §2 substrate)
  - `Scheme.RationalMap.order_units_inv` (L274, §2 substrate)
  - `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_neg` (L488, §4 substrate)
  - `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_sub` (L497, §4 substrate)
- **Declarations blocked (1)**: `rationalMap_order_finite_support` (L325) non-zero branch — genuine mathematical blocker; the statement is stated under `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]` but the proof (Stacks 02RV / Hartshorne II.6.1) requires `[IsNoetherian X]` (= `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]` + `[CompactSpace X]`).
- **Sorry count**: 3 → 3 (unchanged; structural blocker on L249, now relabelled L325 after substrate additions).
- **f = 0 branch cleanup**: refactored to use the new `order_zero` lemma instead of in-line unfolding. Net cleaner code.

## Sections

### Scheme.RationalMap.order_zero (L233)
- **Approach**: `unfold` + `map_zero` (for `Ring.ordFrac _` being a monoid-with-zero hom) + `WithZero.log_zero`. 4 lines.
- **Result**: RESOLVED — axiom-clean ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}).

### Scheme.RationalMap.order_mul_of_ne_zero (L242)
- **Approach**: `unfold` + `map_mul` (monoid-with-zero hom) + `WithZero.log_mul` (which requires both factors nonzero; provided by `map_ne_zero` and the hypotheses).
- **Result**: RESOLVED — axiom-clean ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}).
- **Reuse**: can refactor `principal_hom.map_mul'` body (L484–L504) to invoke this lemma — left as iter-199+ optional cleanup.

### Scheme.RationalMap.order_inv (L258)
- **Approach**: `unfold` + `map_inv₀` (for `Ring.ordFrac _ : K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` and `K` a field/group-with-zero) + `WithZero.log_inv` (which holds for all `WithZero (Multiplicative G)` elements including zero — junk-zero convention).
- **Result**: RESOLVED — axiom-clean ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}). Notably works without `f ≠ 0` hypothesis.

### Scheme.RationalMap.order_units_inv (L274)
- **Approach**: bridge `((u⁻¹ : K(X)ˣ) : K(X)) = (u : K(X))⁻¹` via `simp`, then apply `order_inv`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED — axiom-clean ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}).
- **Reuse**: useful for `principal_hom.map_inv'` analogue if/when bundled.

### Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_neg (L488)
- **Approach**: rewrite via `degree_hom_apply`, invoke `map_neg` on the `AddMonoidHom degree_hom`. 3 lines.
- **Result**: RESOLVED — axiom-clean ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}).

### Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_sub (L497)
- **Approach**: rewrite via `degree_hom_apply` three times, invoke `map_sub` on the `AddMonoidHom degree_hom`. 3 lines.
- **Result**: RESOLVED — axiom-clean ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}).

### rationalMap_order_finite_support L249 → L325 (non-zero branch)
- **Approach 1 — Affine open + minimal primes**: Pick a nonempty affine open `U ⊂ X`, get `R = Γ(U, O_X)` Noetherian integral domain, write `f = a/b ∈ Frac(R) = K(X)`. Bound prime divisors with `Y.point ∈ U` via `Ideal.finite_minimalPrimes_of_isNoetherianRing` on `(a · b) ⊂ R`. **STALLED**: prime divisors with `Y.point ∉ U` correspond to irreducible components of the closed set `X \ U` of codim 1 in `X`. Bounding their cardinality requires `X \ U` to be Noetherian as a topological space — equivalently, `X` must be globally Noetherian (`AlgebraicGeometry.isNoetherian_iff`: `IsNoetherian X ↔ IsLocallyNoetherian X ∧ CompactSpace X`). The current signature has only `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
- **Approach 2 — Dedekind-domain Mathlib analogue**: `IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.Support.finite` gives the analogue for `R` Dedekind, `k ∈ Frac(R)`. Adapting to schemes requires the affine-chart bridge `ord_Y(f) ≠ 0 ↔ height-1 prime in chart-ring divides numerator or denominator`. **STALLED**: requires the same Noetherian-X-as-topological-space gap.
- **Approach 3 — Try to derive `[IsNoetherian X]` from existing hypotheses**: `[IsIntegral X]` + `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]` does NOT imply `[CompactSpace X]` (counter-example: infinite-dim integral locally Noetherian scheme without quasi-compactness). The bridge `[IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]` is independent of compactness. No clean derivation path.
- **Approach 4 — Informal agent consultation**: attempted `archon-informal-agent.py --provider kimi` and `--provider auto`; both returned `API error 401: Invalid Authentication` (Moonshot key in env is stale). No working LLM key available for sketch.
- **Result**: NOT ADDED (sorry preserved from iter-192). Existing comment expanded with a precise structural-blocker analysis (~30 lines) documenting the affine-open / outside-set / Dedekind bridge and the iter-199+ resolution path.
- **Next step**: iter-199+ planner should consider strengthening `rationalMap_order_finite_support`'s typeclass to `[IsNoetherian X]` and propagate to all consumers (`principal`, `principal_apply`, `principal_one`, `principal_hom`, `principal_degree_zero`, `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`, `LinearEquivalence`). The propagation is mechanical for the curve-side consumers because `[IsProper C.hom] + IsAffineHom (?)` over `Spec(.of kbar)` gives `CompactSpace C.left` for free. Per the iter-198 USER directive, those declarations are Route C PAUSED — so the signature strengthening is itself gated.
- **Dead end**: Do not retry the proof under `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]` alone — the statement is mathematically false in that generality (any non-quasi-compact integral locally Noetherian scheme with infinitely many disjoint codim-1 irreducible components is a counter-example).

## Blueprint markers

New declarations added in §2 and §4 of the chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex`. The 6 new lemmas should be added to the chapter informally by the planner (blueprint writes are read-only for provers). The deterministic `sync_leanok` phase will manage `\leanok` markers next iter.

## Mathlib gaps documented

- `[IsNoetherian X]` vs `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]` for `rationalMap_order_finite_support` (the central blocker).
- `Ring.isUnit_iff_ordFrac_one_of_isDiscreteValuationRing` is available for per-Y bridges (DVR stalk case) — can be used for refactoring `order Y u = 0 for u : (stalk Y.point)ˣ` if needed in iter-199+.

## Why I stopped

**Real progress**: 6 axiom-clean substrate lemmas added in §2/§4. Verified via `lean_verify`:
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_zero` — axiom-clean.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_mul_of_ne_zero` — axiom-clean.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_inv` — axiom-clean.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_units_inv` — axiom-clean.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_neg` — axiom-clean.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_sub` — axiom-clean.

**Blocked — alternatives exhausted**: `rationalMap_order_finite_support` L249 non-zero branch genuinely requires `[IsNoetherian X]` (= `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]` + `[CompactSpace X]`), but the existing signature only has `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`. Tried:
1. Direct affine-open + minimal-primes (FAILED — "outside" bound needs global Noetherian).
2. Dedekind-domain HeightOneSpectrum analogue (FAILED — same gap).
3. Type-class derivation of `[IsNoetherian X]` from existing hypotheses (FAILED — no such derivation; counter-examples exist).
4. Informal agent consultation (FAILED — no working LLM API key in env).

Strengthening the signature requires propagating `[IsNoetherian X]` to the (Route C PAUSED) consumers `principal_degree_zero` and `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` — which the USER directive forbids touching this iter. So the resolution path is iter-199+ planner work under USER approval.

**Sorry count**: 3 → 3 (no net change in sorries; pure substrate growth).
